emails.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
emails.body:
Hey! I really appreciate your patience. I hope that you like these
selections!
Let me know which 3 courses your group would like to move forward with.

*Appetizers & Starters*

   1.

   Roasted squash & spinach terrine w/ manchego cream + almonds
   2.

   Butternut squash soup w/ spiced chamomile cream, crispy shallots + onion
   toast
   3.

   Corn bisque w/ burnt onion + thyme buttermilk
   4.

   Cream of cauliflower soup w/ burnt onion + thyme
   5.

   Fall salad w/ wilted spinach, walnuts, caramelized onion vinaigrette,
   dehydrated cranberries or pears, baked brie
   6.

   Fried cauliflower & potato ball w/ herb aioli, local curds, crispy leeks
   7.

   Grilled pears w/ hyssop ricotta, summer pickled vinaigrette + lettuce mix
   8.

   Grilled peppers stuffed w/ ricottaâwalnut blend, burnt maple butter sauce
   9.

   Blooming onion w/ fresh ribeye jerky + horseradish herb cream
   10.

   Mushroom ravioli w/ brown sage butter, creamed mushroom + spinach,
   chestnut crumble

------------------------------
*EntrÃ©es (Main Courses)*

   1.

   Filet mignon w/ confit garlic butter + parsnip purÃ©e, seasonal green
   2.

   Breaded Rosemary Chicken w/ Savory Roasted Apple w/ cauliflower purÃ©e,
   brown butter, seasonal green + local sumac
   3.

   Braised Chicken + Hickory Nut Romesco w/ caramelized celeriac root +
   cabbage, radish
   4.

   Braised lamb w/ brown butter lemon sauce, capers, zucchini + eggplant
   5.

   Braised short rib w/ pumpkin barbecue glaze, grilled vegetable salad,
   slow-cooked sweet potato, yogurt
   6.

   Crispy salmon over farro risotto, roasted pumpkin, sage + wilted greens
   7.

   Pan-seared scallops w/ garden summer garden relish, corn purÃ©e, burnt
   onions + fried potatoes
   8.

   Scallops pan-seared w/ tomato + spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©e
   9.

   Lobster tail w/ lemon beurre blanc, cauliflower purÃ©e, seasonal green
   10.

emails.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
emails.body:
Hi David,
Thanks for sending the new menu options.  I believed I mentioned that our
group would prefer to forgo dessert and have one appetizer and a salad, so
would you please confirm that you'd be ok with this?  Also, given that one
of our gals has a shellfish allergy, we've decided it would be best not to
offer up any of the seafood suggestions, although they all
sound delicious!  Would you consider offering us a choice of two different
entree options, so that those who would like beef could have it and those
who would like chicken could have their choice?  I suspect this would
complicate things a bit and would understand if you would prefer to just
prepare one entree for our group.  Lastly, is the breaded rosemary chicken
a boneless, skinless chicken breast?

I will be away from Sept 15th - 27th, so please hit "reply all" so Lynne
can pick up my slack.  I would like to have this all figured out before I
leave and send you a deposit if you require it, so please let me know about
that as well.

Thank you!
Jodi

emails.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
emails.body:
Hey I'm really sorry for making you wait. I got caught with a horrible case
of Covid and have been bedridden since we last spoke. Thank you very much
for your patience.

Below I have options I created for you, I will make sure there's a secrets
candied bacon snack and cannabis. ð

Just let me know which 3 or 4 options you'd like to move forward with.

__________________________________

Course 1

Kohlrabi & pork dumplings with brown butter, late-summer ponzu, grilled
pepper pickle

Shrimp & pork dumplings with maple-soy sauce, pickled ginger

Course 2

Slow-roasted squash with parmesan cream foam, candied spiced nuts, crispy
shallots

Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic
vinaigrette

Grilled tomato filled with whipped herb ricotta, warm garden broth, crispy
onions

Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives

Course 3

Aged grilled ribeye and butter-poached mini lobster roll with lemon chive
aioli, summer squash, zucchini, onion, rosemary-sherry sauce

Spice-rubbed filet and haddock with Ritz crumb, lemon butter sauce, grilled
tomatoes, warm summer broth, crispy potato pavÃ©

Course 4

Mocha chocolate mousse with seasonal fruit

Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato

Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries

Peach custard tart with white tea gelato

Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm

Chocolate and hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry

emails.subject: Inquiry for your services
emails.body:
Good afternoon,

My husband and I are staying at the airbnb in Norway hosted by John and Courtney September 28th, 29th, and 30th. If you are available during any of those dates, am I able to get a price estimate for a dinner at the airbnb?
Thanks very much for your time,Â Jonique MonteÂ 
Yahoo Mail: Search, Organize, Conquer

emails.subject: Re: Inquiry for your services
emails.body:
Hey Jonique,

Thank you so much for your inquiry! I'm actually under some bizarre timing
right now because I am in middle of moving. With that said I would still
love to take this on! As these dates do fall on the end of the month, if
anything unfortunate comes up I do have my partner that could easily take
over. I'm sure that I'm oversharing a bit but I'd rather to be transparent
right off the bat. ð§âð³

Below is my pricing and every menu is custom. All I ask is that you provide
me any strong dislikes/favorites + allergies. Then I can start building out
a menu towards your style. Please feel free to leave any additional
information such as the occasion, preferences, etc.

Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)
Large Tasting Menu (8â15+ courses) â Custom rates

- Chef Ferragamo

emails.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
emails.body:
Hi David,
Thank you for being so amenable to offering us a choice of entrees.  It is
very much appreciated!   With regards to a chicken option, we would like a
boneless, skinless chicken breast, so wondering if you could do a lemon
chicken or maybe even a picatta?  I currently have only a partial tally of
entree preference; just waiting on two more women to weigh in.  Once I have
their choices, I will let you know.

Also, as I'm currently out of the country, if you'd like a deposit in
advance of our dinner, please reach out to Lynne Pinto as she has
graciously offered to step in for me while I'm away.  We're very much
looking forward to our evening with you!

Jodi

On Wed, Sep 10, 2025 at 10:02â¯PM David Ferragamo  
wrote:

>

> and beef as long as we keep the sides the same. It would be harder to

> you are looking for. That's no problem at all!
>

>> prepare one entree for our group.  Lastly, is the breaded rosemary chicken
>> a boneless, skinless chicken breast?
>>

>> can pick up my slack.  I would like to have this all figured out before I

>> that as well.
>>
>> Thank you!
>> Jodi

emails.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
emails.body:
Haha you're good. Emailing is a lot sometimes! Huge congratulations on
getting married, you must be having a blast! I'll make sure that I only add
to it.

Thanks for the selections, we have everything we need. ð

 I will be providing my own cannabis for the event, which will be just one
serving for the night. If you're interested in having something that you
can take home that will eventually have multiple servings, feel free to
just provide me a gram of concentrate when I get there. The nearest
dispensary is called Blue Lobster, they are well informed + used to
providing the concentrates for my guests. They're very good friends of
mine, you'll be in great hands.

For example, when I do my cannabis dinners the THC serving size can be
anywhere from 1mg to 80mg. One and done. Or what most people opt in for;
you can give me concentrate which I then turn into a tincture and give back
to you. That tincture would be around 700mg total. So you get a lot more
bang for your buck, could be a fun little present for you guys to walk off
with! (I don't purchase the concentrate but I can prepare it for you)

  On Wed, Sep 17, 2025 at 3:00 PM, David Ferragamo  wrote:   Hey Jonique,Â 
Thank you so much for your inquiry! I'm actually under some bizarre timing right now because I am in middle of moving. With that said I would still love to take this on! As these dates do fall on the end of the month, if anything unfortunate comes up I do have my partner that could easily take over. emails.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
emails.body:
Hey David,
Not sure if you saw my email above, but now that we're less than a week
out, I thought I'd circle back with you as I feel there might be a
disconnect here.  We did not expect you to give each person both chicken
and beef.  My intent was to offer the girls either the chicken or the
beef.  Our count is 6 beef entrees and 3 chicken entrees.

We would like to begin our happy hour at 5:30 so given that, what time
would you like to arrive?  Do you need us to do anything in advance of your
arrival?  A couple of us are supplementing the happy hour nibbles, so I
will already have the oven preheated to 350.

Just confirming that the price per person is $115.

If you would like to be paid in cash, we will need advanced warning.
Otherwise, we can pay you via venmo or zelle.  Does that work for you?

We're looking forward to our evening!

Thanks,
Jodi

emails.body:
Hey Jodi,

Thanks for following up, and I appreciate your patience.

Iâve got the headcount at 9 people total (6 beef entrÃ©es and 3 chicken
entrÃ©es). Letâs set it at a flat rate of $1,000 for the evening. You can
send that to me via Venmo: venmo.com/u/DFPrivateChef.

Iâll plan to arrive around 4:30â5:00 to get set up before happy hour at
5:30. Do you prefer dinner service to start closer to 6:00 or 6:30? Either
works fine, and we can always finalize on the day of.

I donât need anything else on your end, and I really appreciate you having
the oven preheated to 350.

Looking forward to the evening!

- David

emails.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
emails.body:
Hi Jodi and Lynne,

As I was going back over my notes, I realized we didnât finalize the
appetizer and salad for your dinner. Everything else is confirmed: filet
with garlic confit butter or chicken piccata for the entrÃ©es, with
cauliflower purÃ©e, green beans, and charred onion as the sides.

When you get a chance, could you please pick one appetizer and one salad
from this list:

1. Roasted squash & spinach terrine with manchego cream and almonds

2. Butternut squash soup with spiced chamomile cream, crispy shallots and
onion toast

3. Corn bisque with burnt onion and thyme buttermilk

4. Cream of cauliflower soup with burnt onion and thyme

5. Fall salad with wilted spinach, walnuts, caramelized onion vinaigrette,
dehydrated cranberries or pears, baked brie

6. Fried cauliflower and potato ball with herb aioli, local curds, crispy
leeks

7. Grilled pears with hyssop ricotta, summer pickled vinaigrette and
lettuce mix

8. Grilled peppers stuffed with ricottaâwalnut blend, burnt maple butter
sauce

9. Blooming onion with fresh ribeye jerky and horseradish herb cream

10. Mushroom ravioli with brown sage butter, creamed mushroom and spinach,
chestnut crumble

My recommendation would be #1 for the appetizer, since it pairs really well
with your entrÃ©es, and #5 for the salad, which ties everything together
nicely. But of course, feel free to choose whichever two sound best to you.

Once you confirm, the full menu will be locked in. Looking forward to it.

David

emails.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
emails.body:
Hi David,
I thought we discussed our appetizer and salad option on the phone quite a
while back (But maybe we didn't) and it remains the same:  for the
appetizer we liked the fried cauliflower and potato ball with the herb
aioli, local curds and crispy leeks.  If possible, we'd like to incorporate
that with our happy hour nibbles, so if possible, could they be ready at
5;30?  For salad, we'd like the fall salad with the wilted spinach.  I
returned home with a whopping head cold, so I will not be joining the girls
this weekend.  I had wanted the chicken piccata, so you only need to make 2
orders of that now.  I think we're good to go now!
Jodi

emails.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
emails.body:
I apologize for forgetting that we had already talked through the appetizer
and salad on the phone, and thank you for reminding me. With 8 guests now,
the adjusted total is $885, which comes to about $110 per person and keeps
the same discount as before.

Iâll be there for 4:30 to get set before your 5:30 happy hour.

I hope you feel better soon, I'm sorry to hear about that. It'll pass
quick!

Thank you for the information Lynne. Looking forward to Saturday!

David

On Thu, Oct 2, 2025, 8:55â¯PM Powellpinto, Lynne  
wrote:

> Hi David, since Jodi wonât be with us at Quietude, I wanted to provide you
> with my cell phone # in case you need to be in touch:   513 307 8716.
>
>
>
> If we are not at the house when you arrive, the code to get in is:  8716.
>
>
>
> Thanks much and see you on Sat!
>
>
>
> Lynne
>
>
>
> *From:* Jodi Hoffmann  
> *Sent:* Thursday, October 2, 2025 8:35 PM
> *To:* David Ferragamo  ; Powellpinto, Lynne   powellpinto.le@pg.com>
> *Subject:* Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
>
>
>
> [EXTERNAL]
>
>
>
> Hi David,
>
> I thought we discussed our appetizer and salad option on the phone quite a
> while back (But maybe we didn't) and it remains the same:  for the
> appetizer we liked the fried cauliflower and potato ball with the herb
> aioli, local curds and crispy leeks.  If possible, we'd like to incorporate
> that with our happy hour nibbles, so if possible, could they be ready at
> 5;30?  For salad, we'd like the fall salad with the wilted spinach.  I
> returned home with a whopping head cold, so I will not be joining the girls
> this weekend.  I had wanted the chicken piccata, so you only need to make 2
> orders of that now.  I think we're good to go now!
>
> Jodi
>
>
>
> On Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 1:51â¯PM David Ferragamo  
> wrote:
>
> Hi Jodi and Lynne,
>
>
>
> As I was going back over my notes, I realized we didnât finalize the
> appetizer and salad for your dinner. Everything else is confirmed: filet
> with garlic confit butter or chicken piccata for the entrÃ©es, with
> cauliflower purÃ©e, green beans, and charred onion as the sides.
>
>
>
> When you get a chance, could you please pick one appetizer and one salad
> from this list:
>
>
>
> 1. Roasted squash & spinach terrine with manchego cream and almonds
>
>
>
> 2. Butternut squash soup with spiced chamomile cream, crispy shallots and
> onion toast
>
>
>
> 3. Corn bisque with burnt onion and thyme buttermilk
>
>
>
> 4. Cream of cauliflower soup with burnt onion and thyme
>
>
>
> 5. Fall salad with wilted spinach, walnuts, caramelized onion vinaigrette,
> dehydrated cranberries or pears, baked brie
>
>
>
> 6. Fried cauliflower and potato ball with herb aioli, local curds, crispy
> leeks
>
>
>
> 7. Grilled pears with hyssop ricotta, summer pickled vinaigrette and
> lettuce mix
>
>
>
> 8. Grilled peppers stuffed with ricottaâwalnut blend, burnt maple butter
> sauce
>
>
>
> 9. Blooming onion with fresh ribeye jerky and horseradish herb cream
>
>
>
> 10. Mushroom ravioli

Hey Chase,

Thanks for reaching out. Unfortunately I'm fully booked up on those dates.
I'm going to  reach out to my Wednesday client, who shouldn't have a
problem switching. I should have an answer for you by the morning, they're
super flexible. That would be the only day I'm available, I hope that
works!

Below is my pricing and every menu is custom. All I ask is that you provide
me any strong dislikes/favorites + allergies. Then I can start building out
a menu towards your style. Please feel free to leave any additional
information such as the occasion, preferences, etc.

Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses - $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses - $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses - $300/person ($600 total) Large Tasting Menu (8-15+ courses) -
Custom rates

- Chef Ferragamo

On Tue, Oct 7, 2025, 4:41â¯PM Garrett, Chase   wrote:

> Here is what she sent me.
>
>
> Weâre so excited to have you cook for us during our honeymoon and wanted
> to share a few preferences to help guide the menu planning.
>
>
> We donât have any dietary restrictions or food allergies. A few favorites
> of ours are tuna, fresh local seafood, and dishes that incorporate fresh
> herbs and spice - we both love bold, flavorful food.
>
>
> There are just a couple of things weâre not big fans of: squid or octopus,
> and raspberries, or cherries.
>
>
> The overall vibe weâre hoping for is a cozy, romantic date night. Weâll
> also be bringing a selection of wines from our recent trip to Napa Valley,
> and would love for the dishes to pair nicely with those (we can send you a
> list, we will have a vintahe chardonnay and likely a Cabernet of red blend).
>
>
> Weâre both foodies and love trying new things, so please feel free to get
> creative with the menu. If possible, weâd appreciate seeing a draft of the
> menu in advance so we can review it and request any small adjustments if
> needed.
>
>
> Thank you so much â weâre really looking forward to the experience!
>
>
> Warmly,

> Kay & Chase

emails.subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] Re: Dinner Quote
emails.body:
Hey, I just wrapped up the menu. Thanks for being patient! Let me know
which 5 you both would enjoy going forward with. I hope you enjoy the
selections. ð

veg
â¢ roasted squash, charred onion and garlic goat cheese, toasted crumble of
fried delicata squash, shallot, toasted almonds, and marigold petals.
â¢ slow-roasted beets, sumac blackberry vinaigrette, marigolds, toasted
pumpkin, goat cheese, candied walnuts
â¢ braised carrots, whipped garlic ricotta, carrot butter, tossed crumb of
brown bread, almonds, herbs, nasturtium flowers+ bella mushrooms
â¢ charred corn, spinach + brined pepper w/ sweet corn puree, parmesan foam
+ crispy garlic
â¢ butternut soup w/ cinnamon cream, delicate chips + brown sage butter
â¢ fried cheese curd + potato balls, w/ leek aoili,

seafood
â¢ spiced tuna, new england ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
â¢ pan-seared seabass, celeriac puree, roasted white acorn squash, basil +
dandelion sauce
â¢ baked halibut+herb crumb, potatoes au gratin, sauted spinach
â¢ sautÃ©ed scallops with crispy pork belly, braised cabbage, apple/pork
broth + brown sage butter
â¢ lobster thermador, lemon butter, herb potatoes + peas
â¢ crispy salmon w/ goat cheese risotto, buttered squash + bean, maple sage
sauce

meat
â¢ braised shirt rib + mushroom ragu, confit garlic manchego foam, crispy
leeks
â¢ crispy pork belly + apple/pear sauce, fried potato bread cake, dandelion
â¢ spiced ribeye, creamy potato + leek, brined pepper + sherry sauce
â¢ crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, carmelized turnips, cauliflower +
brown butter duck jus
â¢ extra crispy chicken slider, Maine relish + garlic honey w/ curly fry +
fancy sauce

dessert
â¢ chocolate lava, concord grape sorbet, marinated plums+magnolia
â¢ pumpkin cheesecake, chocolate crust, pumpkin spice latte gelato
â¢ pear crisp, cinnamon gelato, salted caramel
â¢ chocoflan, blueberry cookies+cream w/ chocolate shell
â¢ pumpkin cinnamon roll w/ vanilla gelato + candied pumpkin

emails.subject: Dinner party on 10/4
emails.body:
Hello David,

Just had to circle back and tell you again how remarkable the meal was that you prepared for us a few weeks ago.  Although we thanked you that evening, I have thought about that beautiful feast many times.  Every 

single bite was outstanding!  The taste was so memorable, the explanation you gave so interesting, and your humble kindness extraordinary.  Not to mention that you left the kitchen cleaner than when you found it!

Endless thanks for all of it!  I cannot imagine how it could have been better.  You are truly in a league all your own.

Wishing you the very best,  with warmest regards,

Sue Trischman

emails.subject: Dinner
emails.body:
Good Morning,
I had sent a message earlier this week but was not sure if it got to you so
figured I would just email.  I was wondering if you had availability for
01/24/26 (Saturday) around 4-5 to prepare a 40th Birthday dinner for my
wife.  We will be staying at an Airbnb for the weekend.  It is located at
75 Regent Hill Rd Conway, NH 03818.  It will be for 2 people. I am
interested in the THC infused options but don't know if that is available.
Regards,

emails.subject: Re: Dinner
emails.body:
Hey Chris,

I did see your dinner inquiry from my website, thanks for reaching out! I
appreciate your patience in my response time.

I do have that date open at the moment. We can certainly get you marked
down for a booking. Every menu I make is custom so just give me about 7-14
days to have it finished. In which I will then send a menu draft with
several options for you to choose from. Normally our guest all eats the
same thing but I can definitely be flexible.

I have a connection for fresh truffles, I think it would be fantastic to
include this as a surprise for your wife. Let's keep it secret maybe! I'll
keep the upcharge as low as I can for truffles. Ive also got a creme brulee
recipe she'll love for the fall.

Thanks for answering all the questions in the form, it will make my menu
building a lot easier. Please let me know which proteins you prefer to see
on the menu (for exmaple: beef, lamb, scallops,) whatever you both like
best. I'll make sure to provide options that best suit you. Also, please
provide any allergies your party maybe have. Thank you!

If there's anything you'd like to add just include it in your response. If
you'd like to set up a phone call, just let me know when you are free.

- Chef Ferragamo

This is my pricing elow:
Couples (Two Guests)

3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)

4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)

5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)

Large Tasting Menu (8â15+ courses) â Custom rates

Specials & Packages

Two-Day Private Dinner for Two â $900 total

emails.subject: Re: Dinner
emails.body:
David,
Thanks for the response and great news that you have the date open.
I would like to choose the 4 course meal and we are ok having the same menu
Great news on the fresh truffles!
For protein I would choose the beef as she is not a big seafood eater
She also loves garlic in pretty much everything so some options with that
would be nice
Talk to you soon

emails.subject: Re: Dinner
emails.body:
Hey I'm so sorry! I sent you an email saying I was working on your options.
It seems like it never sent! I'll hope your options by tomorrow morning.

I greatly apologize!

- Chef Ferragamo

On Fri, Oct 24, 2025, 8:06â¯AM Chris Zografos  
wrote:

> David,
> Thanks for the response and great news that you have the date open.
> I would like to choose the 4 course meal and we are ok having the same menu
> Great news on the fresh truffles!
> For protein I would choose the beef as she is not a big seafood eater
> She also loves garlic in pretty much everything so some options with that
> would be nice
> Talk to you soon
>
>
>

> wrote:
>
>> Hey Chris,
>>
>> I did see your dinner inquiry from my website, thanks for reaching out! I
>> appreciate your patience in my response time.
>>
>> I do have that date open at the moment. We can certainly get you marked
>> down for a booking. Every menu I make is custom so just give me about 7-14
>> days to have it finished. In which I will then send a menu draft with
>> several options for you to choose from. Normally our guest all eats the
>> same thing but I can definitely be flexible.
>>
>> I have a connection for fresh truffles, I think it would be fantastic to
>> include this as a surprise for your wife. Let's keep it secret maybe! I'll
>> keep the upcharge as low as I can for truffles. Ive also got a creme brulee
>> recipe she'll love for the fall.
>>
>> Thanks for answering all the questions in the form, it will make my menu
>> building a lot easier. Please let me know which proteins you prefer to see
>> on the menu (for exmaple: beef, lamb, scallops,) whatever you both like
>> best. I'll make sure to provide options that best suit you. Also, please
>> provide any allergies your party maybe have. Thank you!
>>
>> If there's anything you'd like to add just include it in your response.
>> If you'd like to set up a phone call, just let me know when you are free.
>>
>> - Chef Ferragamo
>>
>>

>>

>>

emails.subject: Re: Dinner
emails.body:
Hey Chris,

I want to thank you again for your patience. With something this important,
being left without updates is understandably frustrating, and Iâm genuinely
sorry for that. I dropped the ball on communication here, and I appreciate
you sticking with me through it.

We are absolutely confirmed for Saturday, January 24th in Conway, and Iâm
really looking forward to cooking for you both.

I also want to properly follow up on the cannabis question you mentioned
early on. I apologize for not addressing this sooner. Iâm not able to serve
or provide THC-infused food in New Hampshire. That said, if you choose to
provide something yourself, Iâm happy to talk through how it could be
incorporated into the evening in a thoughtful way and walk your party
through how to enjoy it alongside the meal.

As a small thank-you for your patience, Iâd like to include fresh truffles
for the dinner.

Iâve left the menu options below. My general suggestion would be two
vegetable courses, one beef course, and dessert, but nothing here is rigid.
If something different sounds better to you, please donât hesitate to bring
it up. You have the final say on the menu, and Iâm happy to make
adjustments, offer recommendations, or tailor things however youâd like.

Thanks again for your understanding. Iâm really looking forward to hearing
your thoughts and making this a great night for your wife.

Best,
Chef Ferragamo

*MENU*

*Veg*

Creamy winter squash soup with caramelized pumpkin, toasted grains and
nuts, brown butter sage cream

Slow-roasted rutabaga with savory hazelnut crumb, fermented garlic honey,
apple cider, brown butter, fine herbs

Winter root vegetable carpaccio with crispy leeks, confit black walnuts,
smoked beef tallow, sumac, garlic shrub vinaigrette, confit garlic, shaved
manchego

Crispy waffle fries tossed with garlic, parmesan, and fresh shaved truffle,
served with house ketchup
-
*Protein*

Beef Wellington with elderberry and confit garlic demi, hasselback carrots,
pea tendrils, radish

Dry-aged ribeye sous vide with butter, thyme, and garlic, seared hard,
finished with confit garlic butter and toasted onions, cauliflower au
gratin, roasted pickled peppers, rosemary, parsley

Braised short rib with potatoes fondant, creamed spinach, crispy fried
garlic, horseradish crÃ¨me
-
*Dessert*

Layered chocolate cake with beet and raspberry, champagne vanilla gelato

Carrot cake French toast with maple frosting, candied walnuts, carrot cake
gelato, brown butter chai maple broth

Mead-poached pears with squash, walnut, and sablÃ© crumb, salted caramel
gelato

july 12 dinner.subject: July 12 Dinner
july 12 dinner.body:
--000000000000988e5905c3fc6621
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="UTF-8"

Hey Kim,

We are just one week away from our event. This email will wrap up all
concluding details.

Please change/confirm details below, thank you.

   1.

   Date: June 12
   2.

   Location of event: Dracut, MA *please specify*
   3.

   Chef Arrival time: 12pm
   4.

   Eating time: 4pm
   5.

   Allergies: None
   6.

   Guest Count: 11

------------------------------

Menu

cheeseboard - 4 cheese, local charcuterie, potato crackers, preserved + raw
veg

scallop w/ tomato, mozzarella, basil

bison w/ eggplant, corn

almond tart w/ berries + vanilla ice cream

Day of Event

We are a small team consisting of 2-3 chefs. Our team must have time to
arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a
short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all
countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter
space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in
which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.

Our team will provide all the equipment, plates and silverware. This does
not include glassware.

IMPORTANT:  Due to Covid-19, we will no longer be collecting silverware
between courses. We do ask that you prepare your guests to hold on to them.
Instead of linen napkins that we will provide, we will provide a single use
napkin that we ask the host to dispose of. Thank you for supporting these
changes as we strive to keep you and our staff safe.

Be well,

Chef Ferragamo

--000000000000988e5905c3fc6621

Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable

july 12 dinner.subject: Re: July 12 Dinner
july 12 dinner.body:
Hi David. My Address is 156 Primrose Hill Rd Dracut MA. I have 2 driveways as my house is sideways to the street. If you park in the back in front of the white fence, you will have direct acess to the Kitchen. Do you need a deposit or would you prefer cash the day of the event? You can contact me directly at 978-771-2974 
    
    
    
    On 06/05/2021 8:00 AM David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com> wrote:
     
    
     
     
    
     
     
     
      Hey Kim,   
      
      We are just one week away from our event. This email will wrap up all concluding details.    
      Please change/confirm details below, thank you.   
      
      
         Date: June 12     
         Location of event: Dracut, MA *please specify*     
         Chef Arrival time: 12pm     
         Eating time: 4pm     
         Allergies: None     
         Guest Count: 11     
      
     
      
      Menu   
      
      cheeseboard - 4 cheese, local charcuterie, potato crackers, preserved + raw veg   
      
      scallop w/ tomato, mozzarella, basil   
      
      bison w/ eggplant, corn   
      
      almond tart w/ berries + vanilla ice cream   
          
      Day of Event   
      
      We are a small team consisting of 2-3 chefs. Our team must have time to arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.   
      
      Our team will provide all the equipment, plates and silverware. This does not include glassware.    
      
      IMPORTANT:  Due to Covid-19, we will no longer be collecting silverware between courses. We do ask that you prepare your guests to hold on to them. Instead of linen napkins that we will provide, we will provide a single use napkin that we ask the host to dispose of. Thank you for supporting these changes as we strive to keep you and our staff safe.    
     
      
      
     
      
      
      Be well,   
      Chef Ferragamo

july 12 dinner.subject: Re: July 12 Dinner
july 12 dinner.body:
Thank you for the follow-up information. Payment will be done day-off in
cash, before or after the meal. Please let us know if you have any
questions or concerns until then. Enjoy your weekend!

- Chef Ferragamo

february 19 italian dinner.subject: February 19 Italian Dinner
february 19 italian dinner.body:
Good morning Melissa,

We are just under two weeks out from your dinner. I wanted to recap your
options. The menu we sent you was our generic Italian menu, consisting of
many classics. After reading your requirements, I'd like to send you a
revised menu we created just for you.
I have you down for nut allergy + limited carbs. I apologize if this
question is offensive, I've met many variants of nut allergies over my 10
years. Is "Pine Nut" part of your list of allergens? Thank you very much.

Choose 4 dishes to confirm your menu. Please don't be hesitant about asking
or changing anything, I'm extremely flexible. We can create anything you're
looking forward to eating.
*Important: **Our team takes all food allergies extremely seriously. Please
be thorough with all allergies. Thank you from our team.* ð

1. Charcuterie Board - 3 meat, 3 cheese, 1 pickled item, 1 fruit, 1 spread,
1 type of cracker
2. Mushroom medley w/ ricotta stuffed zucchini
3. Chicken Pesto + gnocchi w/ zucchini
4. Zucchini, Fried Prosciutto, 5 cheese alfredo, marinara
5. Seafood stuffed Tomato, fontina w/ parsley + sherry sauce
6. Short Rib Ravioli w/ beef demi, cream of spinach, confit garlic peas
7. Saltimbocca (chicken, veal or haddock)
8. Chicken cacciatore
9. Osso Buco w/ creamy polenta, carrots
10. Stuffed pork roulade w/ brown butter sage sauce, spinach, roast pepper
11. Pear caramel, dark chocolate tart
12. Peach sabayon + pumpkin granola
13. Creme brulee + berries
14. Coffee caramel goat cheesecake + chocolate mousse + cherry
15. Apple crisp w/ cinnamon caramel gelato

My apologies for taking a few days to get back to you. We catered a
spectacular Italian event this past weekend, very much looking forward to
yours. I hope you had a great weekend! Looking forward to hearing from you.

- Chef Ferragamo

dinner parties in rockport/camden: june.subject: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16
dinner parties in rockport/camden: june.body:
Hey Hal,

Thank you for the picture of the kitchen. It helps us immesley for our
preparation.
Our cannabis dining is different from what we are allowed on Take-a-chef.
Thank you for showing interest.
Below is information regarding our dinners. Normally the Q+A is included
but I have your feedback already. Please give me 2-3 days and I'll have a
menu with options to refine available.

You requested a 4-course meal, then it started to sound like you wanted
family style once you learned about the cannabis addition.
When we hold our cannabis dinners, absolutely nothing is dosed beforehand.
This allows all guests to be able to choose their threshold or even opt out
completely.
We have a very intricate way of plating and guest interaction that allows
us to track + dose specific plates.
It is very important to us that all guests are allowed the same exact
experience, while also ensuring everyone's safety.

*Rates per person*
Normal | $200/4course, $240/8course, $260/12course
Cannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12course

Guest count: min 5, max 20

*The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes place.

*$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5

*Day of Event*

We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to
arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a
short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all
countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter
space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in
which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.

-Chef Ferragamo

dinner parties in rockport/camden: june.subject: Re: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16

dinner parties in rockport/camden: june.subject: Re: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16
dinner parties in rockport/camden: june.body:
No worries! We can always arrange something in the future.
For the 16, you can feel free to book directly with me. Follow this link DF
Private Che  f and click "Book
Appointment."

I have created two separate menus for you. Let me know if you'd like to
make any changes. Getting your specific requirements is important, thank
you for the patience.
Please note that slight changes could be made due to seasonal opportunity.

*June 9*

cucumber + bell pepper salad / smoked cherry goat cheese / white pine +
black pepper vin / pecan

basil salmon / zucchini + tomato gazpacho

bison or beef / pickled lilac peppercorns / asparagus / eggplant / sweet
onion

rhubarb, strawberry + goat cheesecake / walnut crust

*June 16*

radish, strawberry, tomato, fennel salad / manchego / crispy garlic scapes

poached crab / asparagus + pea / wheat tart / poppy seed dressing

beef / sweet potato / sauteed greens / peppers

sweet corn gelato / beet cocoa crumb / blueberry

It was fun to create these summer menus, we've been in such a winter
braising mode! Looking forward to hearing from you.
-Chef Ferragamo

> Below is information regarding our dinners. Normally the Q+A is included
> but I have your feedback already. Please give me 2-3 days and I'll have a
> menu with options to refine available.
>
> You requested a 4-course meal, then it started to sound like you wanted
> family style once you learned about the cannabis addition.
> When we hold our cannabis dinners, absolutely nothing is dosed beforehand.
> This allows all guests to be able to choose their threshold or even opt out
> completely.
> We have a very intricate way of plating and guest interaction that allows
> us to track + dose specific plates.
> It is very important to us that all guests are allowed the same exact
> experience, while also ensuring everyone's safety.
>
>
> *Rates per person*
> Normal | $200/4course, $240/8course, $260/12course
> Cannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12course
>
>
> Guest count: min 5, max 20
>
> *The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes
> place.
>
> *$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
>
>
> *Day of Event*
>
> We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must

dinner parties in rockport/camden: june.body:
Hey Hal,

I'll gladly serve the soup first, no problem at all!
I have received your submission, thank you. All dinners will be paid 2
weeks prior to the event. I will be contacting you through Facebook
business for payment on May 26 for the first dinner. The following covers
all returns.

   - If guest cancels up to 14 days before the experience: guest will be
   entirely reinboursed
   - If guest cancels between 14 - 7 days prior to the experience: chef is
   entitled to 50% of the reservationsâ value.
   - If guest cancels with less than 7 days in advance: chef is entitled to
   the entire coverage of the experience
   - If chef cancels: guest will be entirely reimbursement

I have 2 local pork farmers waiting on my word! I have created some
pork/chicken courses for you. Please pick the dish you'd like to
move forward with and which menu you'd prefer it to be on.

 1. bbq pork or chicken burnt ends / corn + carmen peppers / summer pickles

2. brined + grilled pork chop or chicken thigh/ husk cherry jam / charred
onion / potato + seasonal green

3. stuffed pork loin or chicken breast / zucchini + bronz fennel / plum

Enjoy your Sunday! Chat soon.

- Chef Ferragamo

dinner follow-up.subject: Dinner Follow-up
dinner follow-up.body:
Hey Lindsey,

Great speaking with you on Instagram, I'm looking forward to creating your
dinner. Here is the link to the Q + A. https://forms.gle/yqBkxBVp3D2ugtxq6

*Rates per person*
Normal | $115/4course, $150/8course, $175/12course
Cannabis | $200/4course, $250/8course, $275/12course [Follow-up Required]

Guest count: min 5, max 20

*The party size must be confirmed up to 7 days before the event takes place.

*$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5

*Day of Event*

We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to
arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a
short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all
countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter
space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in
which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.

- Chef Ferragamo

dinner follow-up.subject: Re: Dinner Follow-up
dinner follow-up.body:
Hi David,

I just filled out the questionnaire, and meant to add in a note on the
protein preference.  I'm not sure if this is possible, please tell me if
it's not (and no worries), but would (2) of the guests be able to do Bison
and (2) Beef - simply because of a preference/taste thing?  We are not a
picky group and excited to eat whatever you put in front of us LOL so just
let me know.

Best

Lindsey

dinner follow-up.subject: Re: Dinner Follow-up
dinner follow-up.body:
Hey Lindsey,

Thank you for your response on the Q+A. The bison vs beef is no issue! I
have a few menu options for you to choose from.
Please just pick (1) from each category. Feel free to let us know if you'd
like to make any changes.

1st| Squash Soup, Brown Butter Sumac, Apple, Puffed Black Rice
1st| Golden Beet Salad, Fermented Husk cherry Vinaigrette, Leafy Greens,
Spiced Squash Seeds, Goat Cheese
1st| Mushroom + Onion Soup, Savory Carrot Toast w/ Smoked Charred Leek
Butter
1st| Squash Salad, Fermented Pear Vinaigrette, Spiced Squash Seed + Pecan
Brittle, Brie

2nd| Chicken Leek Sausage, Pumpkin Gnocchi, Leek Pinot Cream, Sage,
Pecorino
2nd| Chicken Thighs compressed w/ Marinated Apple, Roasted Peppers, Squash,
Chicken Apple Cider Jus

3rd| Bison or Beef Sirloin, Turnip, Cabbage, Mushrooms, Nasturtium
Fermented Garlic Honey, Pickled Pear
3rd| Bison or Beef Sirloin + Braised Shredded, Cabbage, Squash, Vermouth
Rosemary Demi
3rd| Bison or Beef Sirloin, Savory Carrot Bread, Confit Carrot, Leeks, Sour
Cream

4th| Apple, Black Walnut, Vanilla Chai
4th| Beet, Raspberry, Pumpernickel, Dark Chocolate
4th| Pumpkin, Salted Caramel, Cold Brew, Dark Chocolate

We are currently only booking for Saturday + Sunday, starting November 5th.
Please let us know which dates you had in mind.
Thank you for your patience!

- Chef Ferragamo

dinner follow-up.subject: Dinner Follow-up
dinner follow-up.body:
Hey! I just wanted to let you know that I have seen your inquiry, I
apologize for my response time. It's been a very busy week of dinners. I'm
actually on my way to a service right now but just wanted to reach out.

I am available on that date. Every menu I make is custom so I will use all
of your response to make the menu. Definitely let me know if you think you
left anything out!

Hereâs the pricing for groups of three or more:

$155 per person for three courses
$185 per person for four courses
$215 per person for five courses
We also offer large tasting menus, up to 15 plus courses. This would have
different rates.

And just so you can see both formats, hereâs the couple pricing too:
$200 per person for three courses
$250 per person for four courses
$300 per person for five courses

I work closely with local farms, and I also forage and grow a lot of my own
ingredients. I try to keep the majority of each dinner farm-to-table
whenever possible, to represent the community and the season.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

- Chef Ferragamo

farm-to-table dinner.subject: Farm-to-Table Dinner
farm-to-table dinner.body:
Hey Laurie,

Hope your Monday is starting off well!

Thank you for showing interest in our last month's dinner. We hadn't seen
your message until now, our apologies. Fortunately, I will post the new
event this week. I will make sure that I update you on it right away.

- Chef Ferragamo

march 3 dinner.subject: Re: March 3 Dinner
march 3 dinner.body:
Hey man! I think I've narrowed it down! To our top 4!

I don't know what you need for equipment, but I figured I'd tell you what I do have so you know what you'll need to bring! So I have an electric oven/stove top, a vitamix, a kitchen aid stand mixer, an instapot pressure cooker/air fryer that also can sous vide! Plus normal kitchen stuff like rubber spats, whisks spoons and stuff.

Also, I just wanted to verify, in the subject of the email you wrote March 3 Dinner, but it is for March 11! Sorry for being a pain in the ass, I just wanted to make sure!

private dinner | sept 30.subject: Private Dinner | Sept 30
private dinner | sept 30.body:
Hey Anna,

I apperciate your patience as we get your dinner together. Please
confirm/fill out the information below.

Price: $100/per person
Date: Sept 30
Location:
Serving Style: 3-Course Plated
Serving Time: (Chef will arrive 2hrs early.)
Occasion:
Allergies or Restrictions:

All payments will be made upon confirming your booking. I sent over the
discussed menu selections.

1. Lobster Bisque
2. Spiced Roasted Cauliflower + Smashed Potato w/ crispy leeks + leek puree
+ local cheese curds
3. Filet Mignon w/ Compound Butter of confit pear, garlic + oregano
w/ parsnip puree, buttered seasonal green, + radish

- Chef Ferragamo

private dinner | october 17.subject: Private Dinner | October 17
private dinner | october 17.body:
Hey Jenna,

I apperciate your patience as we get your dinner together. Please
confirm/fill out the information below.

Price: $350
Date: Oct 17
Location: 19 Prentiss St, Old Town, ME
Guest Count: 2
Serving Style: 4-Course Plated
Serving Time: (Chef will arrive 2hrs early.)
Occasion: 2 Year Anniversary Dinner
Allergies or Restrictions:

All payments will be made upon confirming your booking. I sent over
previous selections to create a 4-course menu. I revised it to create
courses that lend well with lobster being the main. I hope you enjoy them.
I can always create anything else or give you more options.

1. Squash, Spinach +Almond Terrine w/ Monchego Lemon Sauce
2. Braised Tomato w/ Carmelized Celeriac Soup + Thyme
3. Whole Lobster w/ Breadcrumbed Tail, Butter + Steamed Broccoli (the rest
will be inside shell and cracked)
4. Chocolate Mousse Pomegranate Pie w/ White Chocolate Sorbet

- Chef Ferragamo

60th birthday dinner.subject: 60th Birthday Dinner
60th birthday dinner.body:
Hi David,
  I hope you are well!  I am not sure if you are still using your calendar
app to book, but I went on and looked to see if you had November 12th
available for a dinner for my mother-in laws 60th birthday.  I grabbed the
slot but wanted to make sure you were in fact available?  It would be at my
mother in law's house in Ipswich, MA.

Looking forward to hearing from you,
   Jill Tzortzis

60th birthday dinner.subject: Re: 60th Birthday Dinner
60th birthday dinner.body:
Hey Jill,

I just finished up the November menu. I hope you enjoy it! Just pick 1 from
each, feel free to let me know of any changes you'd like to make.

1.

shaved kohlrabi + green apple salad w/ confit hazelnut, vermouth pickled
shallots, manchego cheese + sumac dill vinaigrette

warmed brie w/ greenhouse greens, confit potato + turnip, shallot,
pomegranate, walnut, birch syrup vinaigrette

roasted beet + turnip + rutabaga salad w/ citrus goat cheese, arugula,
pear, pecan crackers

2.

caramelized celeriac soup w/ thyme buttermilk, granny smith apple
compressed in herb vinegar, rye crouton

pumpkin soup, chamomile buttermilk, caramelized mushrooms + pumpkin seeds,
toasted pumpkin gut + seed oil

potato + leek soup w/ fermented garlic honey, poached egg + crunchy garlic
herbs

classic zuppa toscana + rosemary focaccia

3.

pan-seared duck breast + jus w/ quince jam, turnip puree, burnt onion,
seasonal green

braised beef w/ fondant potato, carrots, onions + snow pea greens

seafood au gratin w/ sauteed seasonal greens, roast pepper + peas

turkey or chicken breast filled w/ stuffing farce, gravy, cranberry sauce,
brussel sprout, green beans, carrot

4.

slow-roasted butternut custard w/ chai granola, preserved peaches, vanilla
bean ice cream + toasted pumpkin seed oil

mead poached + caramelized pears w/ vanilla anglaise, mead caramel, figs,
mint, candied orange peel

chocolate chip + wintergreen mousse tart w/ preserved raspberry

double chocolate chip brownie w/ preserved strawberry + black walnut ice
cream

sunday dinner.body:
Hi David,
  We are looking forward to our dinner on Sunday! I wanted to confirm what
time we are expecting the first course, did we say 6?  Also, what time
should my mother in law expect you to be at her house (I included her in
this email).  Please let her know if there's anything else you need from
her prior to your arrival.  See you Sunday!

A reminder her address is 10 Capeview Road, Ipswich.

Jill

sunday dinner.subject: Re: Sunday Dinner
sunday dinner.body:
Hi David
I wanted to ask if i should set the table with dishes, glassware and
flatware in advance.
Thank you
Mimi Tzortzis

sunday dinner.body:
If you'd like to be served at 6, then I'll arrive at 4pm to set up. Yes,
you can set the table with your own silverware and glassware. I will
provide my own plates and equipment. :)

- David F

possible dinner in new hampshire.subject: Possible Dinner in New Hampshire
possible dinner in new hampshire.body:
Hi Df,

You were highly recommended by Lauren Boccelli as a possible hire for a
private dinner.

Our family will be staying in Alton, New Hampshire from Dec 26th til the
29th and would like to have a night whem dinner is extra special.

It will be roughly 22 people with 6 kids ranging from age 5 to 15 and the
rest are adults.

It will be at a home we arw renting for the dates above.

Menu would need to be gluten free and budget friendly.  We can do sit down
or buffett style.  The great thing is our family loves food and you can get
creative with the menu.  We would like to do this one of the nights we are
staying at the property.

Not sure if you are available and what is your minimum cost to do this
service.

My phone number is 774 526 0148 and looking forward to hopefully work with
you.

Have a wonderful day!

Virginia Hynes

possible dinner in new hampshire.subject: Re: Possible Dinner in New Hampshire
possible dinner in new hampshire.body:
Hey Virginia,

I am already booked for this, my apologies. I book normally 6 weeks out.
Please let me know if you're interested for the future. Thank you!!

- Chef Ferragamo

private dinner - august.subject: Private Dinner - August
private dinner - august.body:
Hey Dana,

Thank you for you patience in my response, as I've been in and out of the
kitchen this past week! I'm thrilled that you're interested in having me as
your private chef. I know we did already have some of the information down.
To make sure everything is perfect, could you please fix/fill out the
following. Will this information, I will follow up with a menu and price
options. Thank you!

   1.

   *Host:*
   - [Dana]
   2.

   *Date:*
   - [Aug 9,10 or 11]
   3.

   *Location:*
   - [Event Location]
   4.

   *Guest Count:*
   - [3-4]
      5.

   *Arrival + Serving Time:*
   *I will arrive 2 hours before dining begins.
      - [When should I arrive?]
      6.

   *Allergies:*
   - [Any allergies I should be aware of?]
   7.

   *Dietary Restrictions:*
   - [Any dietary restrictions?]
   8.

   *Menu Details:*
   *I will craft a seasonal, progressive New England-style menu.
   - Do you have any suggestions or specific ingredients you'd like to
      include?
      - Any dislikes or food to avoid?
   9.

   *Special Requests:*
   - [Anything special you'd like to include?]
   10.

   *Event Theme/Occasion:*
   - [Any theme or special occasion details?]
   11.

   *Feedback/Notes:*
   - [Anything else I should know?]

   12. *How did you hear about about services? *(Optional)

*Cannabis Consumption Questionnaire:*
To help tailor the experience to your preferences, please answer the
following questions:

-

*1. Is there a recreational cannabis store accessible in your area?*
[ ] Yes
[ ] No
-

*2. Have you or your guests consumed cannabis before?*
[ ] Yes
[ ] No
-

*3. How frequently do you or your guests consume cannabis?*
[ ] Rarely
[ ] Occasionally
[ ] Frequently
-

*4. What is your preferred method of consumption?*
[ ] Edibles
[ ] Smoking
[ ] Vaping
[ ] Other: [Please specify]
-

*5. Do you know you preferred dose?*

[ ]Yes
[ ] No
[ ] I don't know.

Just fill in the details and send it back to me. I'm excited to create a
gourmet dining experience that will make this event unforgettable!

Looking forward to hearing from you soon!

Be Regards,
Chef Ferragamo

private dinner - august.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
private dinner - august.body:
Amazing! Thank you for being open to do this! Attached is a word document with the info requested in the last email. 

Warmly,
Dana Epstein

private dinner - august.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
private dinner - august.body:
Thank you for your patience as I craft your mother's birthday menu! Here
are some options I have created for you. I hope you enjoy them! I'm very
flexible, so don't hesitate to ask for any changes. ð

*Please choose one option for each course:*

*1.*

   - Chopped beet, spinach, and charred onion salad with fermented
   blueberry vinaigrette, lime balm, and goat cheese
   - Tomato and arugula salad with charred strawberry balsamic vinaigrette,
   mint, and tarragon
   - Confit potato, kohlrabi, greens, and radish salad with daylily
   vinaigrette

*2.*

   - Watermelon gazpacho with pickled peppers, grilled vegetables, and
   fresh anise
   - Cucumber gazpacho poured beside a salad of marinated cucumber,
   dill-cured salmon, tomato, and feta cheese
   - Corn gazpacho with scallop crudo, tomato jam, smoked buttermilk, and
   basil

*3.*

   - Crispy chicken with a farce of mushroom and ramp, served beside
   rutabaga purÃ©e. Crispy chicken with chicken consommÃ© French-pressed with
   herbs and flowers (table side)
   - Poached lobster with zucchini breadcrumbs, zucchini, asparagus, and
   beurre montÃ©
   - Rack of lamb with dandelion and black walnut herb crust, sautÃ©ed
   vegetables, and mint
   - Crispy duck breast dusted with lilac salt blend, with pear jam,
   celeriac purÃ©e, confit garlic butter kale, and crispy potato

*4.*

   - CrÃ¨me brÃ»lÃ©e tart with blueberry and young mint
   - Melon buckle cake with marinated melon, whipped cream, and mint
   - Chocolate lava cake with strawberry and gelato
   - Cornbread pudding with marinated blueberry and gelato

*Cannabis Addition:*

DF Private Chef cannot supply cannabis, but we are experts in preparing it
for consumption.

Please purchase 1g of concentrate from your nearest dispensary.

   - If you are visiting ATG in Salisbury, please visit ATG Salisbury
    
   .
   - If you'll be in Ogunquit, Silver Therapeutics has a great
selection. Silver
   Therapeutics Berwick
    
   .

When your chef arrives, please make the concentrate available. In return,
we will convert it into an oil-based tincture ready for consumption.

We advise purchasing only concentrate, as it offers a direct conversion for
making edibles. Flower wouldnât provide definite results in time.

If the store you visit has tinctures, we strongly advise not buying them.
We will make a much stronger tincture for the same price! Most store-bought
tinctures don't reach high strength, maxing at 300mg. Any concentrate you
buy will be 70-85% per gram, which can be converted into a 700-850mg
tincture. This gives us full control of the product and leaves you with a
whole tincture when we leave.

private dinner - august.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
private dinner - august.body:
Hi!!

Here are the Choices/ Feedbackâ¦

Wow! Everything sounds delicious, there are a couple things I am not sure
she enjoys..

1.

Chopped beet, spinach, and charred onion salad with fermented blueberry
vinaigrette, lime balm, and goat cheeseâ¦***Not sure if she would enjoy the
charred onion.

Tomato and arugula salad with charred strawberry balsamic vinaigrette,
mint, and tarragonâ¦****Unsure if she likes tarragon. Otherwise the salads
are up her alley.

2. ****I do not think she is a huge fan of gazpacho. I know she prefers
scallops cooked, not raw. She enjoys either grilled (almost burnt salmon).
Not sure if youâd be able to do a tuna crudo, sashimi or tartar? Other apps
she orders a lot is crab cakes and grilled shrimp or shrimp cocktail.

3.  ***She does not like lamb or kale. Loves duck so if that could be made
different probably that if not I would say the poached lobster. She prefers
claw over tail.

   - Poached lobster with zucchini breadcrumbs, zucchini, asparagus, and
   beurre montÃ©
   - Crispy duck breast dusted with lilac salt blend, with pear jam,
   celeriac purÃ©e, confit garlic butter kale, and crispy potato

4. Chocolate lava cake with strawberry and gelato** sounds perfect!

Look forward to hearing from you!
Warmly,
Dana Epstein

private dinner - august.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
private dinner - august.body:
Let me know what you think about these options!

1. cooked, raw kohlrabi salad + grilled tomato salad dressed w/ herb
toasted black walnut finished with a creamy parmesan sauce
or
mixed green salad w/ blueberry vinaigrette, croutons + mozzarella

2. tuna crudo w/ cucumber + radish dressed purple pepper + basil
vinaigrette
or
crab cakes w/ lemon butter sauce + zucchini

3. crispy duck breast served w/ crunchy potatoes, seasonal greens,
preserved + raw stone fruit
or
poached lobster with zucchini breadcrumbs, zucchini, asparagus, and beurre
montÃ©

4. chocolate lava cake with strawberry and gelato

private dinner - august.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
private dinner - august.body:
Yes, this is fine. It won't be too much, no worries at all! I can totally
make it happen. ð

We have you down for a 5 course menu (1 salad, 2 app, 1 entree, 1 dessert
-split)
I would need the final head count by Friday. I want to be sure to hit the
markets!

I normally charge 160/per person for a 4 course meal. Being how the menu is
set up, I won't have to adjust the price.
I'm sorry for making this feel rushed as the event is a few days away. I do
require 50% deposit upon booking, then the rest 2 days before the event
date.

If you have any questions or need assistance, don't hesitate to ask me.

I can accept Venmo or you can use your card on my site.

The amount sue would be 480 or 640.

https://venmo.com/code?user_id=3286910017470464648

https://www.dfprivatechef.com/book-now

private dinner - august.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
private dinner - august.body:
Hi David!!!

Looking forward to tomorrow! I just wanted to give you a heads up that my
mom prefers her duck cooked through, more done medium/well done,  basically
just not any rareness. Otherwise so excited for this!!!!! Wahoo!

Address: 6 Robin Way, Ogunquit ME.

Her house is right next to the Admiralâs Inn (Yellow hotel). There is a
main driveway and then a parking area owned by us to the left of the house,
feel free to park there! My fiancÃ© Mark will be there to greet you and help
you! Any questions feel free to call or text 508-404-4542.

Warmly,
Dana Epstein

dinner booking.subject: Re: Dinner Booking
dinner booking.body:
Hey Joyce,

I just finished up your custom menu. Thank you for your patience as I
wrapped my head around the fall flavors that will be available!!

I really hope you like the selections I created for you. Please let me know
if you have any questions or would like changes! ðð¨âð³

Please choose 1item from each to create a 4 course menu.

1st
Beet salad w/ fermented garlic honey vinaigrette + goat cheese

Kohlrabi salad w/ confit garlic vinaigrette + sourdough croutons

Apple, pecan, roasted squash, mixed greens + citrus vinaigrette

Roasted squash + charred onion terrine w/ parmesan cream + Moroccan almonds

2nd
Corn soup w/ pickled pepper + thyme cream

Creamy autumn soup + mixed veg

Roasted carrot soup + crispy marinated kale crisp

3rd
Grilled steak tips w/ crunchy potatoes + seasonal greens

Braised short rib w/ creamy rutabaga puree, roast pepper + seasonal greens.

Crispy chicken provencal + roasted root veg

Salmon over winter veg risotto, spiced maple + fresh snow pea greens

Crispy duck breast w/ stone fruit gastique, roasted cauliflower + seasonal
greens

Lobster au gratin w/ seasonal greens

4th
Pear crisp w/ salted caramel + gelato

Chocolate lava cake + seasonal fruit

Pumpkin creme brule

Hazelnut + chocolate swirled cake w/ cherry gelato

birthday dinner.subject: Birthday Dinner
birthday dinner.body:
Hey Michelle,

Thank you for the inquiry and patience with my response! I certainly do
dinners in Haverhillâitâs actually my hometown, so itâs always fun to
visit. The 13th works for me, and Iâd love to start planning your
cannabis-infused dinner. Iâll make options based on your preferences.

Feel free to reach out if you have any other details to discuss. ð¨âð³ð

Best,
Chef Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

oct 18 cannabis dinner.subject: Oct 18 Cannabis Dinner
oct 18 cannabis dinner.body:
Hey Shelby,

I appreciate your submission. I'm very sorry about my delayed response! The
website provider I use has been throwing my cannabis submissions in the
spam folder, this is something I'm working on fixing.

I'd love to come cook for your end of season gathering. We can certainly
always work on a collaboration also, I'm always open to this. That sounds
like fun!

I do specialize in modern new england cuisine, I will begin working on a
vegan menu for you. Please don't hesitate to leave any food suggestions or
further preferences. I can make anything you've got in mind! :)

I have you down for a 4-course meal. Please let me know if this is what you
had in mind.

- Chef Ferragamo

autumn dinner.subject: Autumn Dinner
autumn dinner.body:
Hi S+P,

Thank you for your patience, I normally donât take this long. Iâve been
working through a few earlier submissions, and Iâm pleased to say that your
menu was next in the queue. Iâve now had the chance to focus on it and put
together a tailored selection of options for your event.

Please take a look at the attached menu and let me know your final choices
or if youâd like any adjustments. Iâm happy to make any tweaks to ensure
your event is exactly how you envision it.

For the cannabis-infused option, please present us 1g of cannabis
concentrate upon arrival. I will convert the item and make it available for
consumption while we prep. In return, we will make a tincture that is used
during and left with you after our departure.
I highly recommend "rosin." Check out this dispensary nearest to you.
https://greenport.wm.store/?filter%5BanyClientCategories%5D%5B%5D=rosin-concentrates-category-pages

Price: $160 per person

Event Details:

Date: Oct 18

Location: 67 Main Street, Newcastle, ME

Guest Count: 8

Menu: Vegan Autumn Harvest

Arrival Time: (2.5 hours prior to serving)

Serving Time:

Allergies:

Looking forward to hearing from you soon!

-- Choose 4 Items --

1. Rutabaga, apple, potato terrine w/ cashew dill sauce + yarrow shrub

2. Melon carpaccio w/ celery, fennel, coriander vinaigrette, + pili nut

3. Sweet potato + fennel soup w/ caramelized fennel, pickled peppers +
almond cream

4. Fried mushroom + squash fire cider, elderberry powder

5. Braised eggplant + mushroom w/ autumn veg demi-glace, spiced nuts,
crunchy leeks

6. Cabbage filled w/ celeriac root puree, crispy eggplant + herb sourdough
crumb

Desserts:

1. Pears poached in maple syrup, birch w/ candied black walnut ice cream +
chai cake

2. Pumpkin pudding w/ macadamia sweet cream, caramel kettle corn +
chocolate mousse

3. Concord grape + chocolate tart w/ vanilla sorbet

Best regards,
David Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

autumn dinner.subject: Autumn Dinner
autumn dinner.body:
Hey Aaron,

Thank you for your inquiry! Iâve received your message and will start
working an autumn menu for you. Please confirm + fill out the information
below.

Date: Oct 26
Location: 5 Waverly Dr, Dover, NH
Guest Count: 8
Depart Time: 3
Arrival Time: 4:30 (2hr prior)
Serving Time: 6:30
Allergies: -

Any favorite ingredients or strong dislikes?** We specialize in crafting
seasonal, progressive New England-style menus.

Best,
David Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

autumn dinner.subject: Re: Autumn Dinner
autumn dinner.body:
Hi David, wow this sounds amazing!
Could we discuss other available dates you have?

When we contacted you initially we werenât sure of our closing date, and
now a personal family matter has come up as well Iâm not sure when the
whole team will be able to get together but perhaps(and hoping) late
October or early November? We are fairly flexible but I know that that
weekend of October 18 is not going to work.   it also doesnât need to be a
weekend for us if that helps you.

Thanks!!
Shelby

autumn dinner.subject: Re: Autumn Dinner
autumn dinner.body:
Hey Shelby,

Thank you very much. We can certainly do another date. I did see a post on
social media, I greatly apologize for your lose. I wish the best for your
family and friends.

Maybe we can wait till November for the sake of not feeling rushed. Right
now, I do have a ton of availability in November. Weekend and weekdays
included. Please let me which dates sound best for you.

Also. No rush at this time to complete this inquiry.

- David

december dinner.subject: December Dinner
december dinner.body:
Hey Louisa,

Thank you for your inquiry! Iâve received your message and will be getting
back to you shortly to discuss your event details.

Looking forward to connecting soon. ð¨âð³

Best,
David Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

december dinner.subject: December Dinner
december dinner.body:
Hey Louisa,

I'm just seeing your submission. I apologize for my delayed response; I've
been experiencing some technical issues with my site.

I'll start working on a menu for you based on your criteria. My pricing is
$165 per person for parties of five or more, with an increase for smaller
gatherings. For a private initmate dinner for two, the rate is $200. I hope
this finds you well.

Looking forward to hearing from you. ð

Best regards,
David Ferragamo

katherine + benjamin dinner.subject: Katherine + Benjamin Dinner
katherine + benjamin dinner.body:
Hey Susanne,

I'll be looking to arrive tomorrow at around 3:30pm to start preparing for
the elopement service. I'm looking forward the lovely autumn evening!

- Chef Ferragamo

infused birthday dinner party.subject: Infused Birthday Dinner Party
infused birthday dinner party.body:
Good evening,

We came across your website and are intrigued by the experience you
provide. We are hosting a dinner party to celebrate a birthday in a few
weeks (December 7) in Pepperell, MA. There will be eight adults attending.
We are not sure how it all works, but are definitely interested in having a
fun evening with great infused food and drinks.I know that guests will be
requesting different dosing levels. Is this something that fits with your
services?

Stephen & Jenilee

infused birthday dinner party.subject: Re: Infused Birthday Dinner Party
infused birthday dinner party.body:
Hey Stephen,

This sounds like a blast! I'm actually already booked for Dec 7, which is a
huge bummer. I can see if my guest can swap dates since it seems like you
are already set in stone. If you have other dates, feel free to let me
know. Yes, I offer different doses for every diner. I would love to come
cook for y'all. I hope we can work something out. ð§âð³

- Chef Ferragamo

green geans llc: next steps for your fre.subject: Green Geans LLC: Next Steps for Your Freeze-Drying Inquiry
green geans llc: next steps for your fre.body:
--_000_LV3P220MB18538DB293AF69FE110EEA6CA62F2LV3P220MB1853NAMP_
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="Windows-1252"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable

Hi David!

Green Geans LLC specializes in freeze-drying, with extensive experience in processing both meals and individual ingredients. Weve worked with a wide variety of products, including fresh and frozen produce, herbs, shellfish (such as green crab and oysters), and foraged foods like spruce tips and flowers. However, its important to note that some items do not freeze-dry well, including large quantities of butter, oil, and honey, as well as fatty cuts of meat like brisket.

Pricing is made with incoming wet weight of $x per wet pounds. Estimate range from $8/ lbs. - $18/ lbs. We range this based on the services that are needed, this is why we want to get a full understanding of your vision.

Attached are a few pages related to the inquiry. Once they are filled out to reflect your goals and preferences, Ill have a better idea of how to approach packaging with you. Im also curious if youre looking for bulk orders or specific sizing that fits your recipes.

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions. Looking forward to hearing from you!

Cheerio,
-Gina M.

CEO of GREEN GEANS LLC
FREEZE-DRYING SERVICES

A: 90 BRIDGE ST. STE 255 WESTBROOK, ME 04092
P: 207-413-7988  E: greengeans207@gmail.com 
W: www.greengeans.com   IG: @greengeans

in airbnb private dinner in naples, main.subject: In Airbnb Private Dinner in Naples, Maine? - This Friday?
in airbnb private dinner in naples, main.body:
Hello Chef,

My name is Mike McGovern, my wife and I will be visiting an Airbnb in
Naples, Maine this upcoming weekend and was wondering if you might be
available to do a private dinner in our Airbnb for us two on Friday night
in the 6pm range?  It's the Airbnb, located at 36 Brilliant Way, hosted by
Jon, Rose, and Courtney.  We got your name from them.  I know it's last
minute and on a busy hospitality night to boot, but figured it can't hurt
to check.

Please let me know.  Maybe see you Friday night.

Have a great week!

Mike

-- 
Michael McGovern
234 Greens Farm Road
Branford, Connecticut 06405
Cell â 860-450-6072
Michael.McGovern22@gmail.com

airbnb private dinner in naples, maine -.subject: Airbnb Private Dinner in Naples, Maine - Friday the 31st - Food Preferences
airbnb private dinner in naples, maine -.body:
Hey Chef,

It was nice chatting with you on text today.  Just to be clear, as the week
goes on and you're not quite feeling up to it, don't be shy, it won't kill
us to go out to a restaurant.  Anyway, here's our general preferences.

Overall and generally speaking, we're adventurous diners, not still beating
cobra heart adventurous, but like to try new things for sure.  As far as
differing cultural flavors, etc., we're open, but we both dislike curry.
For meats, we like to avoid turkey, pork (except bacon as a flavoring /
accessory), swordfish and octopus.  No organ meats.  We particularly like
beef and lobster.  Open to game meats to a limited / tame degree.  And
since we're Maine tourists, we would particularly like lobster in the mix.
For health reasons no raw meats / fish for me personally, but Nicole (my
wife) can.  While I can not have blatant raw, I can have meats prepared as
rare as you might seem fit / best.

On the vegetable / starch side, we're game for anything.  Also open to any
vegetarian / vegan dishes you might like to offer.

For desserts, almost anything.  We love cakes, cookies, tarts, ice cream,
but Nicole doesn't like things like panna cotta, creme brulee, and like
textured items.  And nothing way way out there like duck feet ice cream.

For the cocktail, we enjoy creative bourbon sours or old fashioneds.  We
also like fancy martini / vodka like drinks.  But really open to anything,
not super experienced with nicely prepared cocktails.

Please let me know how I can pay you.

This is going to be so fun.  Thank you!

Good night.

Mike

dinner for two?.subject: Dinner for two?
dinner for two?.body:
Hello!

My husband and I are renting an airbnb in Harrison Maine next week and I
was wondering what your price was for a weekday dinner for two? Would be a
luxury highlight of our getaway! This would be for Tuesday, Wednesday, or
Thursday of next week (15-17).

Thanks so much,
-Emily

dinner for two?.subject: Re: Dinner for two?
dinner for two?.body:
Hey Emily,

Thanks for reaching out! Iâd love to cook for you two during your stay.

All the menus I create are custom, so if you have any strong dislikes or
must-haves, feel free to let me know. I focus on progressive New
England-style cooking and use a lot of local ingredients, but Iâm happy to
make anything you're in the mood for.

Here are a few options I offer for couples:

$200 per person
3 courses â appetizer, main, and dessert

$250 per person
4 courses â salad, soup, main, dessert

$300 per person
5 courses â small bites, main, and desserts

Iâm currently free on that Wed+Thursday of that week. Let me know what you
prefer and Iâll send over a menu. ð

- Chef Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

dinner for two?.subject: Re: Dinner for two?
dinner for two?.body:
Here are some options I created for you, my apologies for the late message.
I can make any alterations or changes! I threw in one fish dish for fun.

(Please cheese 1 dish from each)
1. Salads
â¢ Wilted greens with candied walnuts, cheese curds, pickled pepper, and a
confit garlic-maple vinaigrette
â¢ Roasted beet and fennel salad with goat cheese and preserved blueberry
vinaigrette
â¢ House Caesar with charred broccolini

2. Starters
â¢ Cream of cauliflower and roasted pepper over seared cauliflower, braised
turnips, and crispy leeks
â¢ Stuffed artichokes with herb breadcrumbs, Manchego, and lemon beurre blanc

3. Mains
â¢ Braised short rib with mushrooms, Madeira, slow-cooked onions, garlic
mashed potatoes, and seasonal greens
â¢ Seared ribeye with leek butter, charred peppers stuffed with roasted
vegetables and goat cheese, crispy leeks, and braised leaf greens
â¢ Rolled beef roulade with garlic scapes and preserved summer giardiniera,
served in a slow-braised spring beef broth
â¢ Baked halibut with roasted cabbage filled with celeriac-garlic mousse,
finished with lemon brown butter and capers

Content-Type: text/plain; charset="Windows-1252"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable

Hi there!

I=92m reaching out to inquire about private chef services for a special eve=
nt we=92re hosting on July 9th at our company property in Tuftonboro, NH : =
https://www.emberescapes.com/

Our team will be gathering at Quietude from July 8th=9610th for a few days =
of leadership development and team bonding. Since we work remotely and typi=
cally only see each other while traveling for events, this retreat offers a=
 rare and meaningful opportunity to connect in person.

On the evening of July 9th, we will be honoring the recipient of our annual=
 =93Grace Award,=94 which recognizes our team=92s MVP. We=92d love to make =
this moment even more special with a beautifully prepared three-course dinn=
er for 18 hospitality professionals and meeting planners.

We=92re looking for a chef who can:

  *   Use our fully equipped kitchen at the property
  *   Plan, cook, and serve a three-course celebratory meal
  *   Optionally prepare breakfast or light items for the following day, if=
 interested (was thinking a few breakfast casseroles that we can pop in the=
 oven)

If this sounds like a good fit, I=92d love to schedule a video call to disc=
uss details further.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

Colleen

[Logo  Description automatically generated] 

Colleen Hartigan
Gathering Producer
colleen@emberbrandfire.com 
508.277.8784

Ember Escapes: Our mountain retreat for offsites and small gatherings 
Follow our work:

on automatically generated]

Hey Colleen,

Thank you so much for everything you provided! Im working on the menu, I'll
make sure to have it ready by the 10th.

I'm going to price the dinner at 100 a person, but normally I do charge
more. I can include my normal pricing just so you know for future
reference, if we decide to take on any endeavors.

As for breakfast I would say I do normally charge as well. Following what
we have discussed, Id be happy to leave a ready-to-eat meal for 20-30 a
head depending on the food.

I'll make some options for each course to narrow down with your team. I'll
also provide a whole separate vegan menu, if you decide you want to go that
way. I don't exactly type up all my menus, since I do have repeat clients
with less formal intentions. So they do vary from BOH to FOH looking.

private chef inquiry - july 9th dinner -.subject: Re: Private Chef Inquiry - July 9th Dinner - Tuftonboro, NH
private chef inquiry - july 9th dinner -.body:
Hey Colleen,

Thank you for your patience as I get into my busy season. I have all the
allergies noted.

I recommend narrowing dinner down to a three-course meal. If youâre excited
about adding any extra courses you see on the menu, just let me know!

As we discussed on the phone, I can do $100 per person for the dinner. This
is a lower rate for me, in hopes that we can turn this into something more
down the line. I'm currently managing a great relationship with a Airbnb
Host who has several properties in Maine, in which I have consistent
bookings with. I'd always be glad to connect you with him as a reference.

For the breakfast and the hiking snack, $25 a person for one option of
each. If youâd like to add more than that, just let me know and Iâll make
it work.

Thank you!

*summer 2025 menu*

*app*

   -

   melon + cucumber gazpacho, local feta
   -

   smoked peppers filled w/ goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic
   vinaigrette
   -

   grilled stone fruit, arugula, fresh ricotta, preserved pepper vinaigrette
   -

   summer salad + blueberry balsamic vin + fresh mozzarella
   -

   cucumber + pea salad w/ brown butter lemon vinaigrette + manchego

*main*

   -

   bbq steak tips, buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato
   salad
   -

   blackberry smoke + braised short rib w/ grilled vegetable salad +
   cornbread
   -

   roasted chicken + herb jus w/ burnt onion, turnips + marinated cherry
   -

   chicken roulade stuffed w/ tomato, basil + asparagus + crispy potato
   -

   crispy duck + peach mostarda, sauteed broccolini, burnt onion + herb
   broth
   -

   haddock + herb crumb w/ sauteed zucchini, pea shoots + basil butter
   -

Hi Colleen,

Dinner is $100 per person. Thereâs no added service or travel fee. Iâm
based in Waterford, Maine, which is just over an hour from you. I also have
places I stay all over New England depending on where my dinners are, so
the travel is no issue for me. This is a normal drive.

The only additional charge is 5.5 percent Maine sales tax.

For service, it will be me and one other chef. We donât hire outside
servers. All food and service is handled directly by us.

For the two guests who donât eat red meat, Iâll prepare chicken. The vegan
guest will get a plated jackfruit dish, and the pescatarian will have a
seafood option. If they have a preference for the type of fish, just let me
know and Iâll make sure they get something theyâll enjoy.

I can also put together a grazing table before dinner. I use local
ingredients, so itâs more involved than a basic cheese board. I can offer
that at $20 per person.

Hereâs the full price breakdown:

Dinner (July 9 for 18 guests)
3 course dinner at $100 per person
Includes food, service for two people, and travel
Maine sales tax at 5.5 percent applies
Dinner subtotal: $1800
Sales tax: $99
Total: $1899

Breakfast and Hiking Snack (July 10)
One breakfast and one snack per guest at $25
Total: $450

Optional Grazing Table (Pre Dinner)
$20 per person
Optional total: $360

Total with Grazing Table: $2709

Let me know if anything needs adjusting and Iâll send over the final
invoice.

-Chef Ferragamo

Hi Colleen,

Thanks so much for these final details! Everything looks great and we are
all set for Wednesday, July 9th. I've got all the timings, guest count of
17, dietary requirements, and logistical details fully noted. I'm really
looking forward to establishing this connection and making this the first
of many dinners with your team. I truly hope we can impress you! ð

I've added the $200 gratuity to your invoice, and the updated version is
attached to this email for your records. Your W-9 form is also attached.

Looking forward to a wonderful event, it's going to be a great time!

Best,
Chef David
