custom dinner options â august 2 in ha.subject: Custom Dinner Options â August 2 in Harrison
custom dinner options â august 2 in ha.body:
Hey Robert,

Thanks for reaching out, and I appreciate you being patient while I catch
up. Iâd love to cook for you during your trip to Harrison.

I attached a sample Summer 2025 menu to give you a feel for what Iâve been
serving lately. This is just a sample Iâve been offering since June. I
mainly make custom menus, but Iâm super flexible. If you see anything on
there that looks good, feel free to pick directly from it, or I can put
together completely new options based on what youâre into. Totally up to
you.

I saw the note about the onion. Weâll make sure to leave it out.

Hereâs the pricing for groups of three or more:

$155 per person for three courses
$185 per person for four courses
$215 per person for five courses
We also offer large tasting menus, up to 15 plus courses. This would have
different rates.

And just so you can see both formats, hereâs the couple pricing too:

$200 per person for three courses
$250 per person for four courses
$300 per person for five courses
We also offer large tasting menus, up to 15 plus courses. This would have
different rates.

I work closely with local farms, and I also forage and grow a lot of my own
ingredients. I try to keep the majority of each dinner farm to table
whenever possible, to represent the community and the season.

Let me know what kind of dishes youâre drawn to or if you want me to send
over a few custom options. Looking forward to hearing what sounds good.

Talk soon,
Chef Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

inquiry.subject: Inquiry
inquiry.body:
Hi Chef David,

I hope you are doing well!=20
I requested to book with you for June 28th through your website, and was wan=
dering if this could be a possibility? I just wanted to check in and see if m=
y request went through.

Thank you!=20

Mia=20

Sent from my iPhone=

inquiry.subject: Re: Inquiry
inquiry.body:
Hi Mia,

Thanks so much for checking in, and I appreciate your patience while I
catch up. Iâd love to cook for you on June 28th for Alanaâs 30th. That
sounds like a fun one.

Iâll start working on some menu options and will have them to you later
this week. I always build menus custom to the group, but if you already
have a direction youâre leaning toward, feel free to let me know.

Hereâs the pricing for groups of three or more:

$155 per person for three courses
$185 per person for four courses
$215 per person for five courses
I also offer large tasting menus, up to 15 plus courses. This would have
different rates.

I work closely with local farms, and I also forage and grow a lot of my own
ingredients. I try to keep most dinners as local and seasonal as possible
to really represent the area and the time of year.

I saw your notes. Weâll avoid crab, lamb, and anything not
pregnancy-friendly. Iâll keep it fun and flavorful without compromising on
safety.

Looking forward to putting something together for you.

Best regards,
Chef Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

inquiry.subject: Re: Inquiry
inquiry.body:
Hi Chef Ferragamo,

Thank you so much for the thoughtful message. We are really excited to work
with you for Alanaâs birthday celebration.

One quick note â one member of our group is vegan, gluten sensitive, and
also has sensitivities to potatoes, sweet potatoes, turmeric, and
watermelon. We would really appreciate accommodations for that. If this
changes the menu for all guests no worries, and disregard the above (that
person is me so no worries haha)

Other than that, we do not have any specific culinary preferences, so
please feel free to create whatever you would like. We trust your
expertise!

We are also still deciding between doing three or five courses. If it is
not too much trouble, would you mind sharing your ideas for both options so
we can choose from there?

Looking forward to seeing what you come up with! Thank you again!

Best regards,
Mia Corrado

inquiry.subject: Re: Inquiry
inquiry.body:
Thank you for gathering your groupâs allergies and preferences. It makes my
job a lot easier.

Iâve attached a custom summer menu built with everything in mind. Thereâs
no crab, lamb, fish, cold cuts, raw foods, or anything unpasteurized. I
also avoided sweet potatoes, turmeric, and watermelon, and Iâve included a
solid vegan and gluten-free option. Totally no problem! Nobody would know
the difference. All the food will be delicious.

Does Alana have any foods sheâs obsessed with? Itâs always fun to throw in
a surprise.

Youâll see the full list of courses below. You can pick one veg, one main,
and one dessert if you're leaning toward a three-course dinner, or choose
up to five dishes from anywhere on the menu. We do suggest limiting the
mains to two just to keep it smooth. If you end up wanting three mains,
that usually comes with an upcharge, but happy to throw it in on the house
if thatâs what you go with. Two desserts is always very fun!

I have you down for a 5:30 serving time. I typically arrive two hours
before to set up.

Let me know what sounds good and Iâll take it from there.

Chef Ferragamo

---

New England Summer
All dishes have vegan versions available. All vegan dishes are cooked in
olive oil. All vegan dairy will be house-made.

VEG
â¢ SautÃ©ed snap pea and broccolini salad with brown butter vinaigrette,
manchego
â¢ Grilled stone fruit, arugula, house-made vegan ricotta, preserved pepper
vinaigrette
â¢ Summer salad with blueberry balsamic vinaigrette and fresh mozzarella
â¢ Chopped beet, spinach, and charred onion salad with black pepper
blueberry vinaigrette, dried lime balm, and goat cheese
â¢ Tomato gazpacho with crispy shallot oil, basil, and pruned blueberry
capers
â¢ Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, and lilac
garlic vinaigrette
â¢ Strawberry carpaccio with charred corn relish, goat cheese, and lots of
herbs
â¢ Tomato, arugula, and herb salad with charred strawberry balsamic
vinaigrette, mint, and tarragon

MAIN
â¢ Spice-rubbed filet with basil butter, grilled tomatoes, warm broth, and
crispy potato pavÃ©
â¢ Aged grilled ribeye with rosemary sherry sauce, summer squash, zucchini,
and onion
â¢ Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach,
and crispy garlic
â¢ Blackberry smoked and braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad and
cornbread
â¢ Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, and asparagus with crispy
potato
â¢ Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, and marinated cherry

DESSERT
â¢ Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust and marinated berries
â¢ Peach custard tart with white tea gelato
â¢ Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm
â¢ Chocolate and hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache and raspberry
â¢ Chocoflan with chocolate chip gelato
â¢ Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit and gelato

On Mon, Jun 16, 2025, 8:54â¯PM Mia Corrado   wrote:

> Hi Chef Ferragamo,
>
> Thank you so much for the though

inquiry.subject: Re: Inquiry
inquiry.body:
Hi Chef Ferragamo,

Thank you so much for sending over these incredible menu ideas â everything
looks absolutely amazing, and so generous! We were all so impressed by the
creativity and variety, and we can already tell this will be such a special
dinner.

I just had a couple of small thoughts. Would it be possible to offer a few
more appetizer options that are not salad-based, just to balance things out
a bit? Everything sounds delicious, and we will probably still pick one or
two of the vegan options you suggested, but no need to worry about
providing vegan and gluten-free options across the board â we are happy to
enjoy the full range of dishes.

Also, if itâs possible, we would love to include a little surprise dessert
â maybe something chocolate and hazelnutâfocused, (ur bread pudding sounds
amazing, but my sister isnât a bread pudding fan sadly ð) since my sister
isnât a huge fan of fruit-forward desserts.

Thank you again â we are so excited and canât wait to see what you create!

inquiry.subject: Re: Inquiry
inquiry.body:
Hi Mia,

I really appreciate your patience while I updated your options. I know the
date is coming up quickly, and I hope you're happy with these. I'd love to
hear your thoughts. I can make any changes you'd like!

Again, any of the main courses you've seen will have a structured vegan
version. For example, the braised short rib would be swapped for a braised
celeriac root, and so on.

I know I got the direction for chocolate and hazelnut, if you have any
berries or fruit in mines that your group loves please let me know.

Veg
â¢ Zucchini roulade with marinated tomatoes and basil
â¢ Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli and buttermilk chive dip
â¢ Grilled brioche toast with whipped stracciatella, peach jelly, smoked
marinated peppers, and crispy shallots
â¢ Honeydew melon and cucumber gazpacho with lilac vinegar, bronze fennel,
fresh mint, and toasted fennel seed oil
â¢ Grilled shishito peppers tossed with smoked tomato butter, served with
giardiniera ponzu
â¢ Grilled flatbread with whipped feta, savory marinated blueberries,
pickled shallot, fresh herbs, and crunchy garlic walnuts

Desserts
â¢ Chocolate mousse tart with hazelnut crust
â¢ Chocolate hazelnut tiramisu
â¢ Ice cream sandwich with hazelnut cookie, chocolate gelato with chocolate
chips
â¢ Nostalgic dirt pudding with hazelnut and chocolate

- Chef Ferragamo

inquiry.body:
Hi Chef Ferragamo,

Thank you so much for putting time and effort into this amazing meal. I
really appreciate all your work you have done in your busy schedule.

Unfortunately, I just heard word from my sister, she explained that she was
a bit worried about having a decadent meal in her last trimester, because
of her sensitive stomach. Iâm so sorry to tell you this last minute, I just
have received this information now.

That being said, I would love to keep in contact with you, in order to hire
you for a meal with less restrictions. Your food looks amazing!

Thank you again,

Mia

private chef dinner - september.subject: Private Chef Dinner - September
private chef dinner - september.body:
Hello David,
I hope this message finds you well and you are having a great summer! I am reaching out as I have a mini-moon booked in Naples, Maine and the Airbnb host provided your contact information regarding hiring a private chef for dinner one evening. I would love to surprise my finance with a really special dinner if you have availability while we are there. We will be staying in Naples Sept 18, 19, and 20th.Â 
Would you be available for a private dinner for two one of those nights? My partner would also love a meal cooked with cannabis if that is available for 1 and a regular meal for the me.Â 
I look forward to hearing from you!
Thank you!

Elisa Perel08-737-2766

private chef dinner - september.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
private chef dinner - september.body:
Hi Elisa,

Thank you for reaching out, I received the wix form as well.
Congratulations on your upcoming mini-moon! I can put September 18th, 19th,
and 20th on hold for you until you decide which date works best.

I do want to apologize, as Iâm unable to conduct any cannabis-related
business in the state of New Hampshire. That said, Iâd still love to cook
for you and create a really special dinner. Does you finance have any
favorite or nostalgia foods that we could surprise them with?

For a private dinner for two, pricing is as follows:
â¢ 3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)
â¢ 4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)
â¢ 5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)

If youâd like, please share any allergies, favorites, or strong dislikes so
I can start shaping the menu around your preferences.

Let me know which date and menu option youâd like to move forward with. Iâm
excited to make your celebration memorable! ð§âð³

- Chef Ferragamo

Hi David, Happy Friday!Â 
Thank you for the quick response! The dinner is actually in Naples, Maine - not New Hampshire - does that change anything with the cannabis dinner? We are staying at an Airbnb in Maine for the nights listed. The kitchen is gorgeous, I can send you the link if that is helpful.Â 
Would you have any sample menus by chance or be available for a phone call to discuss? I am trying to decide between 3 or 4 courses. We do not have any allergies or major dietary restrictions, we love lobster, mussels, steaks, root veggies, broccoli, broccoli rabe, broccolini... I love all veggies, my partner hates "salad" but I love it. Pat doesn't like oysters, clams or shrimp - I love them all.Â 
Can we plan on our last night, Sept 20th for the dinner? Let me know if you have a few minutes next week for a call and I can provide more detail.
Thank you so much! We have never done anything like this before and this will be my surprise wedding gift for my partner. He has been talking about trying to do this since the Airbnb host shared your details and we started looking through your reviews.Â 
Thanks again!
ElisaÂ

private chef dinner - september.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
private chef dinner - september.body:
Hello,
Thank you for this information - super helpful and I am excited! My schedule this Monday, August 18th is pretty flexible - do you have a preferred time?
I did think of one thing we both love - dumplings! And we would both love to watch you prepare them. For dessert, Pat loves mocha flavor and we love chocolate mousse with some funky fruit and/or candied bacon? He loves bacon!
Everything sounds delicious! No salad for the app or veg as Pat hates it - I'm working on that one!Â 
As for mains, lobster, surf and turf would be great. He does not like salmon, scallops, duck or short rib.
We love steak, squash or root veggies, potatoes...I think lobster/surf and turf would be really nice! We also loves haddock.Â 
Does that help at all? This is a first for me so guidance is appreciated. Would it be possible to make something for him with cannabis? Not for me, so I totally understand if that is not possible to do a dish separate.Â 
I look forward to connecting - let me know what time works best!
Thanks!Â 

Elisa Perel508-737-2766

 Thank you very much for your feedback. That makes my menu building a lot more helpful and personal. We can def get him some cannabis with your meal being effected, that's no issue.Â 
I'll make some really good bacon, you're talking to the right guy haha I've got everything else covered. I loved how specific you were, it lets me really hone in on what you'd like!Â

Also feel free to check out my Instagram, I'm always very active. I post my dinners and daily food stuff. It'll give you an idea of the service I provide.Â

I hope you had a great weekend and enjoyed the beautiful weather! Did you have availability for a call this week?Â 
Thank you! Elisa
    On Friday, August 15, 2025 at 03:37:04 PM EDT, Elisa Perel   wrote:  
 
  Hello,
Thank you for this information - super helpful and I am excited! My schedule this Monday, August 18th is pretty flexible - do you have a preferred time?
I did think of one thing we both love - dumplings! And we would both love to watch you prepare them. For dessert, Pat loves mocha flavor and we love chocolate mousse with some funky fruit and/or candied bacon? He loves bacon!
Everything sounds delicious! No salad for the app or veg as Pat hates it - I'm working on that one!Â 
As for mains, lobster, surf and turf would be great. He does not like salmon, scallops, duck or short rib.
We love steak, squash or root veggies, potatoes...I think lobster/surf and turf would be really nice! We also loves haddock.Â 
Does that help at all? This is a first for me so guidance is appreciated. Would it be possible to make something for him with cannabis? Not for me, so I totally understand if that is not possible to do a dish separate.Â 
I look forward to connecting - let me know what time works best!
Thanks!Â 

Elisa Perel508-737-2766

lse covered. I loved how specific you were, it lets me really hone in on wha=
t you'd like!      Sorry for my res=
ponse time. I'm around Thursday if you'd like to chat. =F0=9F=98=8A  My number is 978-

private chef dinner - september.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
private chef dinner - september.body:
Hey I'm really sorry for making you wait. I got caught with a horrible case
of Covid and have been bedridden since we last spoke. Thank you very much
for your patience.

Below I have options I created for you, I will make sure there's a secrets
candied bacon snack and cannabis. ð

Just let me know which 3 or 4 options you'd like to move forward with.

__________________________________

Course 1

Kohlrabi & pork dumplings with brown butter, late-summer ponzu, grilled
pepper pickle

Shrimp & pork dumplings with maple-soy sauce, pickled ginger

Course 2

Slow-roasted squash with parmesan cream foam, candied spiced nuts, crispy
shallots

Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic
vinaigrette

Grilled tomato filled with whipped herb ricotta, warm garden broth, crispy
onions

Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives

Course 3

Aged grilled ribeye and butter-poached mini lobster roll with lemon chive
aioli, summer squash, zucchini, onion, rosemary-sherry sauce

Spice-rubbed filet and haddock with Ritz crumb, lemon butter sauce, grilled
tomatoes, warm summer broth, crispy potato pavÃ©

Course 4

Mocha chocolate mousse with seasonal fruit

Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato

Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries

Peach custard tart with white tea gelato

Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm

Chocolate and hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry

private chef dinner - september.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
private chef dinner - september.body:
Haha you're good. Emailing is a lot sometimes! Huge congratulations on
getting married, you must be having a blast! I'll make sure that I only add
to it.

Thanks for the selections, we have everything we need. ð

 I will be providing my own cannabis for the event, which will be just one
serving for the night. If you're interested in having something that you
can take home that will eventually have multiple servings, feel free to
just provide me a gram of concentrate when I get there. The nearest
dispensary is called Blue Lobster, they are well informed + used to
providing the concentrates for my guests. They're very good friends of
mine, you'll be in great hands.

For example, when I do my cannabis dinners the THC serving size can be
anywhere from 1mg to 80mg. One and done. Or what most people opt in for;
you can give me concentrate which I then turn into a tincture and give back
to you. That tincture would be around 700mg total. So you get a lot more
bang for your buck, could be a fun little present for you guys to walk off
with! (I don't purchase the concentrate but I can prepare it for you)

lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.subject: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.body:
Hi David, my name is Jodi Hoffmann and I'm one of the gals in Lynne's group
renting the Quietude home for the weekend of October 4th.  Since I'm the
"foodie" of the group, Lynne asked me if I'd take on the details for our
dinner that we'd love for you to prepare for us.

Is it possible for you to send me some sample menus that correspond with
your course pricing?  I'd love for the ladies to weigh in with their
thoughts and preferences and the menus would be a huge help.

The only allergy that I'm aware of at the moment is one gal has a
shellfish allergy, but I'll follow up on that.

Thank you!
Jodi
203 218-2312

lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.body:
Hey Jodi, It's nice to meet you! I can certainly give you some sample
menus.
Thank you for the feedback on allergies. Everything will be taken into
consideration with what you decide on. ð

Althought everything is custom, these are many of the dishes I've done this
summer. I'm always taking suggestions, dislikes or favorites in mind while
making these menus. We will be entering spooky season too, I always have
cool things I can make.

- Chef Ferragamo

*Summer Menu*

VEG / APP
â¢ SautÃ©ed snap pea & broccolini salad with brown butter vinaigrette,
manchego
â¢ Grilled stone fruit, arugula, vegan ricotta, preserved pepper vinaigrette
â¢ Summer salad with blueberry balsamic vinaigrette and fresh mozzarella
â¢ Chopped beet, spinach & charred onion salad with black pepper blueberry
vinaigrette, dried lime balm, goat cheese
â¢ Tomato gazpacho with crispy shallot oil, basil, pruned blueberry capers
â¢ Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic
vinaigrette
â¢ Strawberry carpaccio with charred corn relish, goat cheese, herbs
â¢ Tomato, arugula & herb salad with charred strawberry balsamic
vinaigrette, mint, tarragon
â¢ Melon & cucumber gazpacho with local feta
â¢ Cucumber & pea salad with brown butter lemon vinaigrette, manchego
â¢ Grilled Texas toast with whipped herb goat cheese, pepper jelly, raw
zucchini salad
â¢ Grilled & charred eggplant with pickled giardiniera, basil croutons, feta
â¢ Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives
â¢ Bloominâ onion with horseradish aioli, sour cream chive seasoning

MAIN
â¢ Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach,
crispy garlic
â¢ BBQ steak tips with buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato
salad
â¢ Blackberry smoked & braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad,
cornbread
â¢ Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, asparagus, crispy potato
â¢ Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, marinated cherry
â¢ Crispy duck with peach mostarda, sautÃ©ed broccolini, burnt onion, herb
broth
â¢ Haddock with herb crumb, sautÃ©ed zucchini, pea shoots, basil butter
â¢ Salmon with black pepper maple glaze, sautÃ©ed beans, herb rice
â¢ Scallops pan-seared with tomato & spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©e

lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.body:
Hey! I really appreciate your patience. I hope that you like these
selections!
Let me know which 3 courses your group would like to move forward with.

*Appetizers & Starters*

   1.

   Roasted squash & spinach terrine w/ manchego cream + almonds
   2.

   Butternut squash soup w/ spiced chamomile cream, crispy shallots + onion
   toast
   3.

   Corn bisque w/ burnt onion + thyme buttermilk
   4.

   Cream of cauliflower soup w/ burnt onion + thyme
   5.

   Fall salad w/ wilted spinach, walnuts, caramelized onion vinaigrette,
   dehydrated cranberries or pears, baked brie
   6.

   Fried cauliflower & potato ball w/ herb aioli, local curds, crispy leeks
   7.

   Grilled pears w/ hyssop ricotta, summer pickled vinaigrette + lettuce mix
   8.

   Grilled peppers stuffed w/ ricottaâwalnut blend, burnt maple butter sauce
   9.

   Blooming onion w/ fresh ribeye jerky + horseradish herb cream
   10.

   Mushroom ravioli w/ brown sage butter, creamed mushroom + spinach,
   chestnut crumble

------------------------------
*EntrÃ©es (Main Courses)*

   1.

   Filet mignon w/ confit garlic butter + parsnip purÃ©e, seasonal green
   2.

   Breaded Rosemary Chicken w/ Savory Roasted Apple w/ cauliflower purÃ©e,
   brown butter, seasonal green + local sumac
   3.

   Braised Chicken + Hickory Nut Romesco w/ caramelized celeriac root +
   cabbage, radish
   4.

   Braised lamb w/ brown butter lemon sauce, capers, zucchini + eggplant
   5.

   Braised short rib w/ pumpkin barbecue glaze, grilled vegetable salad,
   slow-cooked sweet potato, yogurt
   6.

   Crispy salmon over farro risotto, roasted pumpkin, sage + wilted greens
   7.

   Pan-seared scallops w/ garden summer garden relish, corn purÃ©e, burnt
   onions + fried potatoes
   8.

   Scallops pan-seared w/ tomato + spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©e
   9.

   Lobster tail w/ lemon beurre blanc, cauliflower purÃ©e, seasonal green
   10.

   Butter-poached lobster + lobster bisque w/ asparagus + zucchini roulade,
   crunchy potato

------------------------------
*Desserts*

   1.

   Chocolate lava cake w/ seasonal fruit + gelato
   2.

   Marble cheesecake brownie w/ chocolate mousse + roasted berries
   3.

   Basque cheesecake w/ sweet fern crust + peaches
   4.

   Upside-down pear cake w/ salted caramel + vanilla sumac gelato
   5.

   Pumpkin cinnamon roll w/ cinnamon gelato + candied pears
   6.

   Carrot cake w/ root beer caramel + carrot cake ice cream
   7.

   Vanilla & grape custard w/ chai candied pumpkin granola
   8.

   Baked apples w/ whipped cinnamon ricotta + toasted walnuts
   9.

   Chocolate & hazelnut bread pudding w/ preserved raspberries + candied
   hickory nuts
   10.

   Halloween dish (passion fruit sorbet, black licorice + chocolate cookie)

lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.body:
Hi David,
Thanks for sending the new menu options.  I believed I mentioned that our
group would prefer to forgo dessert and have one appetizer and a salad, so
would you please confirm that you'd be ok with this?  Also, given that one
of our gals has a shellfish allergy, we've decided it would be best not to
offer up any of the seafood suggestions, although they all
sound delicious!  Would you consider offering us a choice of two different
entree options, so that those who would like beef could have it and those
who would like chicken could have their choice?  I suspect this would
complicate things a bit and would understand if you would prefer to just
prepare one entree for our group.  Lastly, is the breaded rosemary chicken
a boneless, skinless chicken breast?

I will be away from Sept 15th - 27th, so please hit "reply all" so Lynne
can pick up my slack.  I would like to have this all figured out before I
leave and send you a deposit if you require it, so please let me know about
that as well.

Thank you!
Jodi

Hi David,
Thank you for being so amenable to offering us a choice of entrees.  It is
very much appreciated!   With regards to a chicken option, we would like a
boneless, skinless chicken breast, so wondering if you could do a lemon
chicken or maybe even a picatta?  I currently have only a partial tally of
entree preference; just waiting on two more women to weigh in.  Once I have
their choices, I will let you know.

Also, as I'm currently out of the country, if you'd like a deposit in
advance of our dinner, please reach out to Lynne Pinto as she has
graciously offered to step in for me while I'm away.  We're very much
looking forward to our evening with you!

Jodi

lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.body:
Hey David,
Not sure if you saw my email above, but now that we're less than a week
out, I thought I'd circle back with you as I feel there might be a
disconnect here.  We did not expect you to give each person both chicken
and beef.  My intent was to offer the girls either the chicken or the
beef.  Our count is 6 beef entrees and 3 chicken entrees.

We would like to begin our happy hour at 5:30 so given that, what time
would you like to arrive?  Do you need us to do anything in advance of your
arrival?  A couple of us are supplementing the happy hour nibbles, so I
will already have the oven preheated to 350.

Just confirming that the price per person is $115.

If you would like to be paid in cash, we will need advanced warning.
Otherwise, we can pay you via venmo or zelle.  Does that work for you?

We're looking forward to our evening!

Thanks,
Jodi

lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.body:
Hi Jodi and Lynne,

As I was going back over my notes, I realized we didnât finalize the
appetizer and salad for your dinner. Everything else is confirmed: filet
with garlic confit butter or chicken piccata for the entrÃ©es, with
cauliflower purÃ©e, green beans, and charred onion as the sides.

When you get a chance, could you please pick one appetizer and one salad
from this list:

1. Roasted squash & spinach terrine with manchego cream and almonds

2. Butternut squash soup with spiced chamomile cream, crispy shallots and
onion toast

3. Corn bisque with burnt onion and thyme buttermilk

4. Cream of cauliflower soup with burnt onion and thyme

5. Fall salad with wilted spinach, walnuts, caramelized onion vinaigrette,
dehydrated cranberries or pears, baked brie

6. Fried cauliflower and potato ball with herb aioli, local curds, crispy
leeks

7. Grilled pears with hyssop ricotta, summer pickled vinaigrette and
lettuce mix

8. Grilled peppers stuffed with ricottaâwalnut blend, burnt maple butter
sauce

9. Blooming onion with fresh ribeye jerky and horseradish herb cream

10. Mushroom ravioli with brown sage butter, creamed mushroom and spinach,
chestnut crumble

My recommendation would be #1 for the appetizer, since it pairs really well
with your entrÃ©es, and #5 for the salad, which ties everything together
nicely. But of course, feel free to choose whichever two sound best to you.

Once you confirm, the full menu will be locked in. Looking forward to it.

David

> works fine, and we can always finalize on the day of.
>
> I donât need anything else on your end, and I really appreciate you having
> the oven preheated to 350.
>
> Looking forward to the evening!
>
>
> - David
>

>> arrival?  A couple of us are supplementing the happy hour nibbles, so I
>> will already have the oven preheated to 350.
>>
>> Just confirming that the price per person is $115.
>>
>> If you would like

lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
lynne pinto's dinner on 10/4.body:
Hi David,
I thought we discussed our appetizer and salad option on the phone quite a
while back (But maybe we didn't) and it remains the same:  for the
appetizer we liked the fried cauliflower and potato ball with the herb
aioli, local curds and crispy leeks.  If possible, we'd like to incorporate
that with our happy hour nibbles, so if possible, could they be ready at
5;30?  For salad, we'd like the fall salad with the wilted spinach.  I
returned home with a whopping head cold, so I will not be joining the girls
this weekend.  I had wanted the chicken piccata, so you only need to make 2
orders of that now.  I think we're good to go now!
Jodi

and salad on the phone, and thank you for reminding me. With 8 guests now,
the adjusted total is $885, which comes to about $110 per person and keeps
the same discount as before.

I hope you feel better soon, I'm sorry to hear about that. It'll pass
quick!

Thank you for the information Lynne. Looking forward to Saturday!

David

inquiry for your services.subject: Inquiry for your services
inquiry for your services.body:
Good afternoon,

My husband and I are staying at the airbnb in Norway hosted by John and Courtney September 28th, 29th, and 30th. If you are available during any of those dates, am I able to get a price estimate for a dinner at the airbnb?
Thanks very much for your time,Â Jonique MonteÂ 
Yahoo Mail: Search, Organize, Conquer

inquiry for your services.subject: Re: Inquiry for your services
inquiry for your services.body:
Hey Jonique,

Thank you so much for your inquiry! I'm actually under some bizarre timing
right now because I am in middle of moving. With that said I would still
love to take this on! As these dates do fall on the end of the month, if
anything unfortunate comes up I do have my partner that could easily take
over. I'm sure that I'm oversharing a bit but I'd rather to be transparent
right off the bat. ð§âð³

Below is my pricing and every menu is custom. All I ask is that you provide
me any strong dislikes/favorites + allergies. Then I can start building out
a menu towards your style. Please feel free to leave any additional
information such as the occasion, preferences, etc.

Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)
Large Tasting Menu (8â15+ courses) â Custom rates

- Chef Ferragamo

Hey Chase,

Thanks for reaching out. Unfortunately I'm fully booked up on those dates.
I'm going to  reach out to my Wednesday client, who shouldn't have a
problem switching. I should have an answer for you by the morning, they're
super flexible. That would be the only day I'm available, I hope that
works!

Below is my pricing and every menu is custom. All I ask is that you provide
me any strong dislikes/favorites + allergies. Then I can start building out
a menu towards your style. Please feel free to leave any additional
information such as the occasion, preferences, etc.

Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses - $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses - $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses - $300/person ($600 total) Large Tasting Menu (8-15+ courses) -
Custom rates

- Chef Ferragamo

[external] re: dinner quote.body:
Hey, I just wrapped up the menu. Thanks for being patient! Let me know
which 5 you both would enjoy going forward with. I hope you enjoy the
selections. ð

veg
â¢ roasted squash, charred onion and garlic goat cheese, toasted crumble of
fried delicata squash, shallot, toasted almonds, and marigold petals.
â¢ slow-roasted beets, sumac blackberry vinaigrette, marigolds, toasted
pumpkin, goat cheese, candied walnuts
â¢ braised carrots, whipped garlic ricotta, carrot butter, tossed crumb of
brown bread, almonds, herbs, nasturtium flowers+ bella mushrooms
â¢ charred corn, spinach + brined pepper w/ sweet corn puree, parmesan foam
+ crispy garlic
â¢ butternut soup w/ cinnamon cream, delicate chips + brown sage butter
â¢ fried cheese curd + potato balls, w/ leek aoili,

seafood
â¢ spiced tuna, new england ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
â¢ pan-seared seabass, celeriac puree, roasted white acorn squash, basil +
dandelion sauce
â¢ baked halibut+herb crumb, potatoes au gratin, sauted spinach
â¢ sautÃ©ed scallops with crispy pork belly, braised cabbage, apple/pork
broth + brown sage butter
â¢ lobster thermador, lemon butter, herb potatoes + peas
â¢ crispy salmon w/ goat cheese risotto, buttered squash + bean, maple sage
sauce

meat
â¢ braised shirt rib + mushroom ragu, confit garlic manchego foam, crispy
leeks
â¢ crispy pork belly + apple/pear sauce, fried potato bread cake, dandelion
â¢ spiced ribeye, creamy potato + leek, brined pepper + sherry sauce
â¢ crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, carmelized turnips, cauliflower +
brown butter duck jus
â¢ extra crispy chicken slider, Maine relish + garlic honey w/ curly fry +
fancy sauce

dessert
â¢ chocolate lava, concord grape sorbet, marinated plums+magnolia
â¢ pumpkin cheesecake, chocolate crust, pumpkin spice latte gelato
â¢ pear crisp, cinnamon gelato, salted caramel
â¢ chocoflan, blueberry cookies+cream w/ chocolate shell
â¢ pumpkin cinnamon roll w/ vanilla gelato + candied pumpkin

dinner party on 10/4.subject: Dinner party on 10/4
dinner party on 10/4.body:
Hello David,

Just had to circle back and tell you again how remarkable the meal was that you prepared for us a few weeks ago.  Although we thanked you that evening, I have thought about that beautiful feast many times.  Every 

single bite was outstanding!  The taste was so memorable, the explanation you gave so interesting, and your humble kindness extraordinary.  Not to mention that you left the kitchen cleaner than when you found it!

Endless thanks for all of it!  I cannot imagine how it could have been better.  You are truly in a league all your own.

Wishing you the very best,  with warmest regards,

Sue Trischman

Hey Chris,

Thanks for reaching out about this dinner inquiry and all the information.
I do have this open, I can certainly mark them down for that date. This
service would be a $1550. I'm looking to forward to setting this up!

- Chef Ferragamo

Hereâs your standard dinner pricing:
Groups of 3 or More
3 Courses: $155/person
4 Courses: $185/person

dinner.subject: Dinner
dinner.body:
Good Morning,
I had sent a message earlier this week but was not sure if it got to you so
figured I would just email.  I was wondering if you had availability for
01/24/26 (Saturday) around 4-5 to prepare a 40th Birthday dinner for my
wife.  We will be staying at an Airbnb for the weekend.  It is located at
75 Regent Hill Rd Conway, NH 03818.  It will be for 2 people. I am
interested in the THC infused options but don't know if that is available.
Regards,

dinner.subject: Re: Dinner
dinner.body:
Hey Chris,

I did see your dinner inquiry from my website, thanks for reaching out! I
appreciate your patience in my response time.

I do have that date open at the moment. We can certainly get you marked
down for a booking. Every menu I make is custom so just give me about 7-14
days to have it finished. In which I will then send a menu draft with
several options for you to choose from. Normally our guest all eats the
same thing but I can definitely be flexible.

I have a connection for fresh truffles, I think it would be fantastic to
include this as a surprise for your wife. Let's keep it secret maybe! I'll
keep the upcharge as low as I can for truffles. Ive also got a creme brulee
recipe she'll love for the fall.

Thanks for answering all the questions in the form, it will make my menu
building a lot easier. Please let me know which proteins you prefer to see
on the menu (for exmaple: beef, lamb, scallops,) whatever you both like
best. I'll make sure to provide options that best suit you. Also, please
provide any allergies your party maybe have. Thank you!

If there's anything you'd like to add just include it in your response. If
you'd like to set up a phone call, just let me know when you are free.

- Chef Ferragamo

This is my pricing elow:
Couples (Two Guests)

3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)

4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)

5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)

Large Tasting Menu (8â15+ courses) â Custom rates

Specials & Packages

Two-Day Private Dinner for Two â $900 total

dinner.body:
David,
Thanks for the response and great news that you have the date open.
I would like to choose the 4 course meal and we are ok having the same menu
Great news on the fresh truffles!
For protein I would choose the beef as she is not a big seafood eater
She also loves garlic in pretty much everything so some options with that
would be nice
Talk to you soon

dinner.subject: Re: Dinner
dinner.body:
Hey Chris,

I want to thank you again for your patience. With something this important,
being left without updates is understandably frustrating, and Iâm genuinely
sorry for that. I dropped the ball on communication here, and I appreciate
you sticking with me through it.

We are absolutely confirmed for Saturday, January 24th in Conway, and Iâm
really looking forward to cooking for you both.

I also want to properly follow up on the cannabis question you mentioned
early on. I apologize for not addressing this sooner. Iâm not able to serve
or provide THC-infused food in New Hampshire. That said, if you choose to
provide something yourself, Iâm happy to talk through how it could be
incorporated into the evening in a thoughtful way and walk your party
through how to enjoy it alongside the meal.

As a small thank-you for your patience, Iâd like to include fresh truffles
for the dinner.

Iâve left the menu options below. My general suggestion would be two
vegetable courses, one beef course, and dessert, but nothing here is rigid.
If something different sounds better to you, please donât hesitate to bring
it up. You have the final say on the menu, and Iâm happy to make
adjustments, offer recommendations, or tailor things however youâd like.

Thanks again for your understanding. Iâm really looking forward to hearing
your thoughts and making this a great night for your wife.

Best,
Chef Ferragamo

*MENU*

*Veg*

Creamy winter squash soup with caramelized pumpkin, toasted grains and
nuts, brown butter sage cream

Slow-roasted rutabaga with savory hazelnut crumb, fermented garlic honey,
apple cider, brown butter, fine herbs

Winter root vegetable carpaccio with crispy leeks, confit black walnuts,
smoked beef tallow, sumac, garlic shrub vinaigrette, confit garlic, shaved
manchego

Crispy waffle fries tossed with garlic, parmesan, and fresh shaved truffle,
served with house ketchup
-
*Protein*

Beef Wellington with elderberry and confit garlic demi, hasselback carrots,
pea tendrils, radish

Dry-aged ribeye sous vide with butter, thyme, and garlic, seared hard,
finished with confit garlic butter and toasted onions, cauliflower au
gratin, roasted pickled peppers, rosemary, parsley

Braised short rib with potatoes fondant, creamed spinach, crispy fried
garlic, horseradish crÃ¨me
-
*Dessert*

Layered chocolate cake with beet and raspberry, champagne vanilla gelato

Carrot cake French toast with maple frosting, candied walnuts, carrot cake
gelato, brown butter chai maple broth

Mead-poached pears with squash, walnut, and sablÃ© crumb, salted caramel
gelato

dinner.subject: Re: Dinner
dinner.body:
Thanks for your selections. We can certainly do a creme brulee and gelato
for you. If there's any flavors you both prefer, just let me know. Here are
some options:

Creme brulee flavors: vanilla beans, eggnog, pumpkin, or chocolate

The gelato will vary depending on what you pick, but im open to suggestions
too

I'll make sure there's no raspberry.

I have your truffles noted, don't you worry! I was going to include them
with the entree you picked regardless. My apologies for not implying that.

Thank you very much for your inquiry. I currently have this spot available.

*The chef will arrive 1.5hr prior to serving time.*
Sun, Dec 28 . 6:00 PM - 9:00 PM
36 Brilliant Way Naples, ME, 04055, US

Guest Count: 2 (assuming)

For couples, pricing is:
3 courses â $225 per person ($450 total)
4 courses â $275 per person ($550 total)
5 courses â $325 per person ($650 total)

I was wondering if you have any favorite dishes, ingredients, or flavors
you love, or if thereâs anything youâve been craving lately.

Let me know what feels right, along with any allergies or strong dislikes.
Iâm looking forward to hearing from you!
